title: Senior Manager, Content & Product Marketing
description:
Summary of the Role:

As Senior Manager of Content & Product Marketing at Maze, you'll be the architect of our content engine and product marketing strategy, creating compelling narratives that drive pipeline generation and revenue growth for our AI-powered cybersecurity platform. This is a unique opportunity to join as one of the early GTM team members of a well-funded startup building at the intersection of generative AI and cybersecurity, where you'll build our content function from the ground up while establishing product marketing practices that scale with hypergrowth.

You'll take full ownership of content strategy and execution—from working with security experts to create technical content in non-traditional formats, to crafting product positioning and sales enablement materials that accelerate deal velocity. Your success will be measured by revenue impact and pipeline generation, not content volume or vanity metrics. This role is perfect for someone who thinks creatively about content beyond traditional B2B playbooks, has strong security domain expertise or can quickly get up to speed, and wants the opportunity to own both content and product marketing as we scale from early traction to market leadership.

Within a year, you'll grow into a Director role leading a team as we expand our content and product marketing capabilities to support our next phase of growth.

Your Contributions to Our Journey:

  • Pioneer Non-Traditional Content Formats: Create and execute breakthrough content strategies that move beyond standard blogs and ebooks—think podcasts, video series, technical exploit-style content, and other creative formats that establish Maze as a thought leader while generating qualified pipeline through storytelling that resonates with technical security buyers

  • Build Player-Coach Content Engine: Execute directly on content creation by working with security experts and internal engineering teams to translate complex AI and security concepts into compelling narratives, while simultaneously developing the strategy and processes that will scale our content function as we grow

  • Own Product Marketing and Positioning: Define and evolve our product messaging, positioning, and go-to-market strategy for our agentic vulnerability management platform, ensuring every product launch and feature release drives measurable customer adoption and revenue impact

  • Drive Sales Enablement Excellence: Develop comprehensive sales enablement materials including battle cards, competitive positioning, demo scripts, product training, and customer success stories that accelerate deal velocity and improve win rates across our sales organization

  • Lead Strategic Marketing Initiatives: Support and contribute to high-impact projects including our website relaunch and Q1 remediation product launch, bringing product marketing discipline to ensure successful execution from concept through launch

  • Scale Through Attention to Detail: Establish and maintain exceptionally high quality standards across all marketing outputs—from planning product launches to ensuring every comma is in the right place—setting the bar for excellence as we scale

  • Partner Cross-Functionally: Work in close partnership with sales leadership, product teams, and engineering to ensure marketing efforts directly support revenue goals, with daily collaboration across US and European time zones to maintain alignment and momentum

What You Need to Be Successful:

  • Creative Content Mindset: 5-8 years in B2B SaaS marketing with proven track record creating non-traditional content that breaks from standard B2B playbooks—we want to see examples of novel formats you've pioneered (podcasts, video series, unconventional campaigns) that drove meaningful business impact, not just "I wrote blogs and ebooks"

  • Security Domain Expertise or Fast Learning Ability: Strong understanding of cloud security, vulnerability management, CVEs, and security workflows, OR demonstrated ability to quickly master complex technical domains and translate them into compelling content. Ideally you can discuss technical security concepts fluently with engineering teams and customers—this is a top priority for success in the role.

  • Dual Marketing Experience: Hands-on experience in both content creation and product marketing execution—you can come from either background but must have proven success in both functions, with examples of building content strategies AND launching products that drove pipeline and revenue

  • High-Growth Marketing Background: Track record at high-growth companies where you've built innovative campaigns with progressive marketing leadership, demonstrating ability to think differently about marketing and execute creative approaches that others might not approve

  • Exceptional Attention to Detail: Meticulous standards for quality across all work—from strategic planning of product launches to ensuring perfect execution of every deliverable—you understand that details matter and consistently hold yourself to the highest bar

  • Player-Coach Capability: Comfort creating content strategies and product marketing programs from scratch while also being hands-on with execution—you're 100% player-coach at this stage, not someone who just manages freelancers or delegates execution

  • Confidence with Humility: Ability to instill confidence in experienced sales teams and collaborate with strong-willed executives while remaining a genuine team player—you're knowledgeable and decisive without being an asshole

  • Revenue-Focused Mindset: Experience tying marketing activities directly to pipeline generation and revenue outcomes, with ability to set and measure success metrics that matter to the business, not just activity-based KPIs

  • Nice to Haves:

    • Experience at vulnerability management, risk platforms, or technical detection tool companies (EDR, NDR, SIEM)

    • Background in backend or infrastructure engineering with transition to product marketing

    • Track record creating video content, podcasts, or multimedia campaigns that generated measurable business impact

    • Experience with AI/ML product marketing or agent-based systems

    • Previous startup experience scaling marketing from Series A through hypergrowth

Why Join Us:

  • Ambitious Challenge: We're using generative AI (LLMs and agents) to solve some of the most pressing challenges in cybersecurity today. You'll be building the marketing engine at the cutting edge of this field, creating content and positioning that defines a new category of AI-powered security solutions.

  • Expert Team: We are a team of hands-on leaders with experience in Big Tech and Scale-ups. Our team has been part of the leadership teams behind multiple acquisitions and an IPO.

  • Impactful Work: Cybersecurity is a force for good - helping to stop cyber attacks ultimately helps deliver better outcomes for all of us. Your content and positioning will directly help security teams discover and adopt solutions that protect organizations worldwide.

  • Build an AI-Native Company: We're building a new company in the AI era with the opportunity to design everything from the ground up - you'll pioneer AI-assisted marketing practices while building content and product marketing functions that scale with hypergrowth.

  • Rapid Leadership Growth: Clear path to Director of Content & Product Marketing within a year, building and leading a team as we scale. Significant equity upside, direct partnership with experienced marketing leadership, and the opportunity to define our category through breakthrough content and positioning.

title: Join our Content Team - Careers at Netflix
description: ENTERTAINING THE WORLDMore than 20 Oscars…Over 130 Emmys…47 Golden Globes and counting… As well as scores of other prestigious accolades, from BAFTAs to Peabodys. The number of our award-winning films, scripted and unscripted TV series, documentaries, and animation titles grows every year. This success is fueled by compelling storytelling and characters that Netflix identifies, secures, develops, and produces on a critical and commercial scale that audiences around the globe have never seen. To entertain the world, we have three content-focused teams: 1) Content Development & Acquisition, 2) Content Production, and 3) Content Production Operations. Together, these teams help deliver the exciting content that keeps our millions of members around the world on the edge of their seats every hour of the day.Content Acquisition & Content CreativeGreat stories can come from anywhere We look for them everywhere through our Content Acquisition and Content Creative teams. Our creative executives in Content Acquisition forge dynamic partnerships with some of the world’s best storytellers, giving them a platform to bring their visions to life. Netflix also recognizes that great programming already exists, and our Content Creative team members strategically identify and procure programming that will enthrall our audiences. To ensure our content is all-engrossing, we work to ensure its formats are all-encompassing, from TV series, films, and documentaries, to kids programming, talk shows, reality series, and other experimental formats. And as great storytelling knows no borders, our Content Acquisition and Content Creative teams also work to find and thrilling programming that transcends country and region in our mission to entertain the world.VIEW ROLES IN CONTENT DEVELOPMENT & ACQUISITIONContent ProductionProducing Exciting Content Producing exciting content is a multi-step and multifaceted undertaking that relies on three groups working in close collaboration at Netflix: Production Management Post Production VFX Production Management oversees and coordinates logistics and operations of Netflix Originals, ensuring projects are completed on time, on budget, and to the highest creative, legal, technological, and safety standards. Bringing all our world-class content past the finish line are team members of Post Production Management, who supervise all activities of a project after primary filming has completed so the final product meets not just Netflix’s high standards, but our audiences’ high expectations. And employees in Visual Effects (VFX) visually enhance stories by integrating generated imagery and live-action footage to create stunning characters and settings that would otherwise be challenging, dangerous, expensive, or impossible. Also making the seemingly unproducible possible are members of Virtual Production, who bring digital worlds to life through pre-visualization of scenes, virtual set design, real-time rendering of scenes, and more.VIEW ROLES IN CONTENT PRODUCTIONContent Operations & InnovationSupporting Production Of Our Content Supporting production of our content is our Content Operations & Innovation group. Under this organization are the following teams: Creative Services Studio Management Services Content Risk Management Sustainability Business Operations Creative Talent Engagement & Development Industry Relations and Partnerships They partner with our Production Management and Post Production Management groups to ensure that production processes run efficiently. From technical support, workflow optimization, and tool development to data management, quality control, and infrastructure support, Content Operations & Innovation is redefining the content creation process every day. Through their innovations, the group is also rewriting what it means to be the studio of tomorrow, facilitating high-quality content.VIEW ROLES IN CONTENT OPERATIONS & INNOVATIONEyelinevfx studios powered by netflix We are thrilled to help power Eyeline. Together, we’re blending decades of award-winning craft, cutting-edge virtual production, and innovation to push the boundaries of what’s possible in storytelling. title: Senior Footwear Project Designer, Design Accelerator
description: Who We Are: Since 1906, New Balance has empowered people through sport and craftsmanship to create positive change in communities around the world. We innovate fearlessly, guided by our core values and driven by the belief that conventions were meant to be challenged. We foster a culture in which every associate feels welcomed and respected, where leaders and creatives are inspired to shape the world of tomorrow by taking bold action today. JOB MISSION: At New Balance Footwear Design, our mission is to unlock the full potential of our team’s creativity. The Senior Footwear Project Designer, Design Accelerator will support in the rapid creation of footwear prototypes as a part of our Design Accelerator function. This person will work with other footwear designers, taking selected initial sketch work and refining the designs into fully factory-ready concepts. Relying on their considerable design experience, they will use their knowledge of factory processes, sole specification standards, and upper constructions to expedite the first prototype. They will serve as the liaison between product designer, 3D designer, materials designer and product developer. As someone who interacts heavily with the factories and development teams, they will also serve as a mentor on footwear design best practices to the wider design organization. MAJOR ACCOUNTABILITIES: SKETCH REFINEMENT: Create quick sketches as may be needed to elaborate on the initial concept. Collaborate with designer and product manager to gain alignment on initial proto direction/ Bring added realism through Vizcom materialization or Gravity Sketch study models. UPPER QUICK-SPEC: Upper spec creation is a key component of design communication to the factory and central to this role. Collaborate closely with materials design team. Have a central understanding of appropriate materials selection to achieve design intent, cost targets, durability and sustainability requirements. Create initial shell pattern when required. ‘Owns’ the techpack creation and revision throughout initial first prototype phase. Assists in the transition of that techpack to the inline design team after first proto. SOLE MODEL CREATION: Creates initial sketches of outsole design, when needed. Communicates to 3D designer base form intent, last selection, and key section details. Works with 3D designer and product designer to refine 3D models before prints are created. COLLABORATION & PROBLEM SOLVING: Act as a liaison between Design Development and PM ensuring alignment and smooth communication. Able to navigate ambiguity with confidence, using resourcefulness to solve problems to clarify design intent. Work with a high level of urgency and ownership to meet deadlines and maintain project momentum. REQUIREMENTS FOR SUCCESS: Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Design or other footwear-related design field (product, graphics, or fashion) Proficiency with Adobe Creative Suite tools a must. 3D experience with Gravity Sketch, Rhino, etc. and AI experience such as Vizcom or Midjourney are a bonus 5+ of footwear design experience, preferably working in the Lifestyle category Strong project management skills and team collaboration Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously Boston, MA Headquarters - (NB) Only Pay Range: $117,400.00 - $152,000.00 - $186,600.00 Annual (actual base pay varying based upon, but not limited to, relevant experience, time in role, internal equity, geographic location, and more.) Regular Associate Benefits Our products are only as good as the people we hire, so we make sure to hire the best and treat them accordingly. New Balance offers a comprehensive traditional benefits package including three options for medical insurance as well as dental, vision, life insurance and 401K. We also proudly offer a slate of more nontraditional perks – opportunities like online learning and development courses, tuition reimbursement, $100 monthly student loan support and various mentorship programs – that encourage our associates to grow personally as they develop professionally. You’ll also enjoy a yearly $1,000 lifestyle reimbursement, 4 weeks of vacations, 12 holidays and generous parental leave, because work-life balance is more than just a buzzword – it’s part of our culture. Temporary associates are provided three options for medical insurance as well as dental and vision insurance and an associate discount. Part time associates are provided 401k, short term disability, a yearly $300 lifestyle reimbursement and an associate discount. Flexible Work Schedule For decades we have fostered a unique culture founded on our values with a particular focus on in-person teamwork and collaboration. Our North American hybrid model encourages rich in-person experiences, showcasing our commitment to teamwork and connection, while maintaining flexibility for associates. New Balance Associates currently work in office three days per week (Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday). Our offices are fully open, and amenities are available across our North American office locations. To continue our focus on hybrid work we have introduced “Work from Anywhere” (WFA) for four weeks per calendar year. This model will help us enhance our culture while continuing to maintain elements of flexibility. Equal Opportunity Employer New Balance provides equal opportunities for all current and prospective associates to ensure that employment, training, compensation, transfer, promotion and other terms, conditions and privileges of employment are provided without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, handicap, genetic information and/or status as an Armed Forces service medal veteran, recently separated veteran, qualified disabled veteran or other protected veteran, or any other protected status. At New Balance, our job is to aid athletes in their pursuit of excellence, whether that means helping professional athletes set records and win medals, or propelling everyday athletes to achieve a new PR, run their first 5K or just to live a more healthy and active lifestyle. Our products are the perfect blend of function and fashion, giving you the performance technology you need and the style you want. Our associates around the world bring these goals to life with their high-level skills and creativity. While we offer the best product on the market, we’re also equally committed to giving back. So while we look to succeed, we believe in ensuring others have the same opportunity.

description: Who We Are: Since 1906, New Balance has empowered people through sport and craftsmanship to create positive change in communities around the world. JOB MISSION: The Footwear Designer for Global Football will provide creative & tactical design expertise for the Footwear category with a focus against In-Line, Market SMU & Athlete propositions. Providing consumer & athlete first design, storytelling & creation solutions to enable delivery of a world class football footwear proposition. We offer hybrid working and a flexibility to working hours to accommodate for a healthy work life balance. Ideal candidates must be based in the North-West of England as this role requires 3 days on site at our EMEA Head office in Warrington. MAJOR ACCOUNTABILITIES: Translate football specific consumer & athlete needs, preference & ambitions into a premier product proposition that is viable & credible in the highest forms of the game Place the consumer experience at the center of category/concept storytelling, with an emphasis on credible, meaningful narratives that drive consumer engagement Able to source a wide range of inspiration & influences to deliver strong curated applications of creativity Develop powerful and compelling product presentations to articulate and inspire adoption. Consumer centric approach to ensure relevant insight is collected & translated across concept, design & engineering to validate & inform creative decision making Leverage & interpret Brand DNA, Seasonal influence and category positioning to drive a consumer right proposition. Explore and implemention of 3D computational design methods and tools to enhance both product accuracy and saliency of the creative vision Creative Graphic ideation and delivery that is realized through newly sourced & developed engineering methods Create technical CAD drawings & schematics to enable Development & vendor partners to realise and complete creative ambition in-line with category objectives High level coherence of seasonal product adjustments to ensure accurate, timely amends are captured, actioned & communicated Partner with Development counterparts throughout seasonal intervals & stage gates to ensure aligned focus against seasonal category/product objectives Foster global vendor partnerships (with Development counterparts) to ensure creative outlook is understood, viable and aligns to creative, consumer & category objectives Work closely with the Product Management team to support the creation & publication of collateral at each Global gate/touchpoint (Key Stories, VLR, GTM, KAP, Pre-Line). title: Senior 3D Motion Designer
description: <h3>The Role<br></h3><p>You are an experienced 3D designer, your days are spent relentlessly authoring ideas, building and rendering high-quality static and moving 3D imagery and environments, and inspiring an exceptional quality of work in the team around you.</p><h3>Your Responsibilities</h3><p></p><ol><li><p>You generate excellent ideas and know how to articulate them expertly both visually and verbally. You run creative catch-ups and idea generating sessions to actively increase the quality of thought in your team.</p></li><li><p>You spend a significant amount of time designing, have a strong understanding of, and can create briefs for: mood boarding and visual R&amp;D, modelling and material building, lighting and cinematography, look development for photorealistic and abstract/experimental projects.</p></li><li><p>You collaborate intimately with the team around you, learning from them and supporting junior staff. You are able to follow direction from the senior team.</p></li><li><p>You can construct client presentations and can present work eloquently.</p></li><li><p>You assist with and can translate our immersion and strategic phases of work into 3D execution.</p></li><li><p>You demonstrate a deep knowledge of branding and the creative practitioners within it. You consistently deliver work for the most impactful companies of today and the founders defining tomorrow.</p></li><li><p>You demonstrate ownership of your role and can both take and action feedback from your manager to progress within it and beyond.</p></li><li><p>You have a strong awareness of the time required to complete 3D tasks. You are able to successfully manage your own time, calendar and meetings.</p></li><li><p>You can take part and own a 3D production pipeline from one-off static assets to fully CG videos. Experience with multi-machine rendering, render farms and servers will be taken into account.</p></li><li><p>You have a willingness to work in person in our London studio and are open to travel globally, from Stuttgart to San Francisco.</p></li><li><p>You have a great understanding of Cinema4D and Redshift as a day-to-day workflow, but can stretch onto other 3D software and render engines like Blender and Octane respectively. After Effects knowledge for compositing and post effects is a minimum requirement.</p></li><li><p>Experience on the following will be taken into account: building 3D assets for web experiences (ie Three.js), use of real-time gaming engines, knowledge of Houdini, NeRFs/gaussian splats/photogrammetry workflows.</p></li><li><p>You're keen to find performance enhancements and opportunities with the integration of AI into your workflow, whilst retaining creative agency over the output of your work.</p></li></ol><h3>Our Responsibilities</h3><p></p><ol><li><p>We will ensure you have meaningful and regular access to your manager to support your personal development and career trajectory.</p></li><li><p>We will actively reward performance with promotions, pay increases and a discretionary annual bonus of up to 10% of salary to all employees.</p></li><li><p>We ensure Koto is an equal environment for all its employees.</p></li><li><p>We will support you in taking family leave and offer significant paid maternity and paternity.</p></li><li><p>We will actively encourage you to take 25 days holiday yearly, in addition to UK bank holidays (on average eight), one additional holiday day per year (capped at 5 additional days), and a studio closure between Christmas and New Year.</p></li><li><p>We will contribute an additional 3% to anyone wanting to engage in the Scottish Widows pension scheme.</p></li><li><p>We offer private medical insurance via Vitality Health and a monthly ‘employee wellness’ benefits package via Juno of £75 per month.</p></li><li><p>We offer access to YCN and fund relevant training opportunities to any employee looking to develop themselves and career.</p></li><li><p>We reward five years of service with £5,000 and five days holiday. We reward ten years of service with £10,000 and four weeks holiday.</p></li><li><p>We will give you the opportunity to work from any the four koto studios globally, with appropriate planning and application.</p></li></ol><h3>Our Culture</h3><ul><li><p>At Koto we encourage our team to be ‘rationally optimistic’ about the potential of our work, our clients, their businesses, and the power of brand.</p></li><li><p>We pursue the best briefs, want to inspire meaningful work, and for those interested provide an opportunity to make the work of their lives. We are actively cultivating leadership, culture and spaces to encourage this.</p></li><li><p>We work in person with an optional WFH day on Friday, and an additional twenty days remote a year. We believe this produces the best work, mentoring opportunities, collective learning and culture.</p></li><li><p>We encourage autonomy in each member of our team, allowing them to manage their own time, hours and personal commitments appropriately to complete work.</p></li></ul>

Privacy policyDate of publication: 07-06-2024We at Koto manage our employer branding and recruitment process through our career site (the “Career Site”), and by using a related applicant tracking system.In this privacy policy, we explain how we process your personal data if: You visit our Career Site (you being a “Visitor”) You connect with us via our Career Site, to create a profile with us and receive information about current or future vacancies with us (you being a “Connecting Candidate”) You apply for a position with us, via our Career Site or a third party service (you being an ”Applying Candidate”) We collect information about you from other parties, sites and services, since we believe your profile is of interest for our current or future vacancies (you being a “Sourced Candidate”) We receive information about you from our employees or partners, since they believe your profile is of interest for our current or future vacancies (you being a “Referred Candidate”) We receive information about you from a Candidate, who lists you as their reference (you being a “Reference”). This privacy policy also describes what rights you have when we process your personal data, and how you can exercise these rights.When we use the term “Candidate” in this privacy policy, we are referring to each of Connecting Candidates; Applying Candidates; Sourced Candidates; and Referred Candidates, unless it’s stated otherwise.1. About processing of personal dataPersonal data is all information that can be directly or indirectly linked to a living, physical person. Examples of personal data are: name, e-mail address, telephone number and IP address. Processing of personal data is any automated use of personal data - such as collecting, creating, analyzing, sharing, and deleting personal data.There are laws and regulations on how companies may process personal data, so-called data protection laws. Different data protection laws apply to different types of use of personal data, and in different parts of the world. An example of a data protection law that is relevant for our use of your personal data, as described in this privacy policy, is the EU Data Protection Regulation (2016/679, “GDPR”). Most obligations under the GDPR apply to the so-called data controller. A data controller is the entity that decides for which purposes personal data will be processed, and how the processing will be executed. The data controller can use a so-called data processor. A data processor is an entity that is only allowed to process personal data as instructed by the data controller, and may not use the personal data for its own purposes.We are the data controller when we process your personal data as described in this Privacy policy.2. What personal data do we process?All individuals Device information - If you visit our Career Site, we will collect information about your device, such as IP address, browser type and version, session behaviour, traffic source, screen resolution, preferred language, geographic location, operating system and device settings/usage. Technical and statistical data - If you visit our Career Site, we will collect technical and statistical data about your use of the site, such as information about which URLs you visit, and your activity on the site. Communications data - We will collect and store your communication with us, including the information you provided in the communication. This may include the content of emails, video recordings, messages on social media, the information you add to your account with us, surveys, etc. Contact details - Such as your name, email address, telephone number and physical address. Candidates Data from interviews, assessments and other information from the recruitment process - Such as notes from interviews with you, assessments and tests made, salary requirements. Information in your application - Such as your CV, cover letter, work samples, references, letters of recommendation and education. Information in your public profile - Meaning the information we collect about you from public sources related to your professional experience, such as LinkedIn or the website of your current employer. Information provided by references - Meaning the information we receive from our employees or partners who refer you to us, or by the persons you have listed as your references. 3. Where do we receive your personal data from?All individuals From the Career Site. If you visit our Career Site, we collect technical and statistical information about how you use the Career Site, and information from your device. Directly from you. Most of the information we process about you, we receive directly from you, for example when you apply for a position with us or connect with us. You can always choose not to provide us with certain information. However, some personal data is necessary in order for us to process your application or provide you the information you request to get from us. References From the person for whom you are a reference. If a Candidate lists you as their reference, we will collect your contact details from the candidate to be able to contact you. Candidates From public sources. We may collect personal data about you from public sources, such as LinkedIn or the website of your current employer. From our references. We may receive information about you from our employees or partners (such as recruitment service providers), when they believe your profile is of interest for our current or future vacancies. From your references. If you provide us with references, we may collect information about you from them. Data we create ourselves or in cooperation with you. Information about your application and profile is usually created by us, or by us in cooperation with you, during the recruitment process. This may for example include notes from interviews with you, assessments and tests made. 4. We share your personal data with our suppliers who provide services and functionality in our employer branding- and recruitment process. For example, this includes recruitment service providers and the supplier of our Career Site and related applicant tracking system. Our group companies. We share your personal data with our group companies, when they provide us services and functionality to our employer branding- and recruitment process, such as access to particular systems and software. Companies providing cookies on the Career Site. If you consent to it, cookies are set by other companies than us, who will use the data collected by these cookies in accordance with their own privacy policy. You can find information about which cookies this applies to in our Cookie Policy. To authorities and other public actors - when we are ordered to do so. We will share your personal data with authorities and other public actors when we have a legal obligation to do so. To parties involved in legal proceedings. If needed to protect or defend our rights, we share your personal data with public authorities or with other parties involved in a potential or existing legal proceeding. This can for example be in case of discrimination claims.Mergers and acquisitions etc. In connection with a potential merger, sale of company assets, financing, or acquisition of all or part of our business to another company, we may share your personal data to other parties involved in the process. 6. On what legal bases do we process your personal data?To be able to process your personal data, we need to have a so-called legal basis. A legal basis is a reason for processing the personal data that is justified under the GDPR.When we process your personal data for the purposes described in this Privacy Policy, the legal basis we rely on is normally that the processing is necessary for our legitimate interest in being able to recruit talent with the relevant competence for us. We have concluded that we have a legitimate interest in being able to perform the personal data processing for this purpose; that the processing is necessary to achieve that purpose; and that our interest outweighs your right not to have your data processed for this purpose. You can contact us for more information about how this assessment was made. See Section 9 and 10 below for our contact information.There may be specific circumstances when the processing is only performed if and when you provide your consent to the processing. This is for example the case if we propose to record an interview with you. Please see Section 9 below for more information about your right to withdraw your consent. 7. When do we transfer your personal data outside of the EU/EEA, and how do we protect it then?We always strive to process your personal data within the EU/EEA area. However, some of our service providers process your personal data outside of the EU/EEA. We also use suppliers whose parent company, or whose subcontractor’s parent company, is based outside the EU/EEA. In these cases, we have taken into account the risk that the personal data may be disclosed to countries outside the EU/EEA, for example because of an authority request.In cases where another recipient of your personal data (as described in Section 5 above) is based outside the EU/EEA, this will also mean that your personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA. When we, or one of our suppliers, transfer your personal data outside the EU/EEA, we will ensure that a safeguard recognized by the GDPR is used to enable the transfer. We use the following safeguards: A decision by the EU Commission that the country outside of the EU/EEA to which your personal data is transferred has an adequate level of protection, which corresponds to the level of protection afforded by the GDPR. In particular, we rely on the EU Commission’s adequacy decision for the US via the so-called EU-US Data Privacy Framework, and the adequacy decision for the UK. Entering into the EU Commission’s standard clauses with the recipient of the personal data outside the EU/EEA. This means that the recipient guarantees that the level of protection for your personal data afforded by the GDPR still applies, and that your rights are still protected. When your personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA, we also implement appropriate technical and organizational safeguards, to protect the personal data in case of a disclosure. Exactly which protective measures we implement depends on what is technically feasible, and sufficiently effective, for the particular transfer.If you want more information about the cases in which your personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA you can contact us using the contact details in Section 9 and 10 below.8. title: Senior Designer
description: <h3>The Role</h3><p>You are an experienced designer, your days are spent relentlessly authoring ideas, designing identities and inspiring an exceptional quality of work in the team around you.</p><h3>Your Responsibilities</h3><ol><li><p>You generate excellent ideas and know how to articulate them expertly both visually and verbally. You run creative catch-ups and idea generating sessions to actively increase the quality of thought in your team.</p></li><li><p>You spend a significant amount of time designing, have a strong understanding of, and can create briefs for: symbolism, typography, color, illustration, iconography and art-direction. You can compose all of these components into complete world-class brand identities, inspire and lead others to the same result, and practically support their design where needed.</p></li><li><p>You collaborate intimately with the team around you, learning from them and supporting junior staff. You are able to follow direction from the senior team.</p></li><li><p>You can construct client presentations and can present work eloquently.</p></li><li><p>You assist with and can translate our immersion and strategic phases of work into design execution.</p></li><li><p>You demonstrate a deep knowledge of branding and the creative practitioners within it. You consistently deliver work for the most impactful companies of today and the founders defining tomorrow.</p></li><li><p>You demonstrate ownership of your role and can both take and action feedback from your manager to progress within it and beyond.</p></li><li><p>You have a strong awareness of the time required to complete design tasks. You are able to successfully manage your own time, calendar and meetings.</p></li><li><p>You have a willingness to work in person in our Culver City studio and are open to travel globally.</p></li><li><p>You have a great understanding of Figma, InDesign, Illustrator, Photoshop, and Pitch.</p></li></ol><h3>Our Responsibilities</h3><ol><li><p>We will ensure you have meaningful and regular access to your manager to support your personal development and career trajectory.</p></li><li><p>We will actively reward performance with promotions, pay increases and a discretionary annual bonus of up to 10% of salary to all employees.</p></li><li><p>We ensure Koto is an equal environment for all its employees.</p></li><li><p>We will support you in taking family leave and offer significant paid maternity and paternity.</p></li><li><p>We will actively encourage you to take 25 vacation days yearly, in addition to US national holidays (on average 10), and a studio closure between Christmas and New Year.</p></li><li><p>We offer a 401k that you can contribute to and Koto matches up to 4%.</p></li><li><p>We deploy a monthly ‘employee wellness’ benefits package via Juno of $105 per month.</p></li><li><p>We offer access to YCN and fund relevant training opportunities to any employee looking to develop themselves and career.</p></li><li><p>We reward five years of service with $5,000 and five days holiday. We reward ten years of service with $10,000 and four weeks holiday.</p></li><li><p>We will give you the opportunity to work from any of the five Koto studios globally, with appropriate planning and application.</p></li></ol><h3>Our Culture</h3><ul><li><p>At Koto we encourage our team to be <strong>‘rationally optimistic’</strong> about the potential of our work, our clients, their businesses, and the power of brand.</p></li><li><p>We pursue the best briefs, want to inspire meaningful work, and for those interested provide an opportunity to make the work of their lives. We are actively cultivating leadership, culture and spaces to encourage this.</p></li><li><p>We work in person with an optional WFH day on Friday, and an additional two weeks remote a year. We believe this produces the best work, mentoring opportunities, collective learning and culture.</p></li><li><p>We encourage autonomy in each member of our team, allowing them to manage their own time, hours and personal commitments appropriately to complete work.</p></li></ul>

title: Senior Motion Designer
description: <h3>The Role<br></h3><p>You are an experienced motion designer. Your days are spent relentlessly authoring ideas, designing animation systems and inspiring an exceptional quality of work in the team around you.</p><h3>Your Responsibilities</h3><ol><li><p>You generate excellent ideas and know how to articulate them expertly both visually and verbally. You run creative catch-ups and idea generating sessions to actively increase the quality of thought in your team.</p></li><li><p>You spend a significant amount of time designing, have a strong understanding of, and can create briefs for: animated brand assets, motion systems, character animation, broadcast toolkits and art-direction. You can compose all of these components into complete world-class brand identities, inspire and lead others to the same result, and practically support their design where needed.</p></li><li><p>You collaborate intimately with the team around you, learning from them and supporting junior staff. You are able to follow direction from the senior team.</p></li><li><p>You can construct client presentations and can present work eloquently.</p></li><li><p>You assist with and can translate our immersion and strategic phases of work into design execution.</p></li><li><p>You demonstrate a deep knowledge of branding and the creative practitioners within it. You consistently deliver work for the most impactful companies of today and the founders defining tomorrow.</p></li><li><p>You demonstrate ownership of your role and can both take and action feedback from your manager to progress within it and beyond.</p></li><li><p>You have a great understanding of After Effects, Premiere and Figma. Provable experience in Cinema 4D + Redshift workflows are not mandatory but will be highly valued.</p></li><li><p>Experience on the following will be taken into account: motion design for interactive web and product experiences (i.e. Rive or Three.js), use of real-time gaming engines, cel animation.</p></li><li><p>You have a strong awareness of the time required to complete motion design tasks. You are able to successfully manage your own time, calendar and meetings.</p></li><li><p>You have a willingness to work in person in our London studio and are open to travel globally, from Stuttgart to San Francisco.</p></li><li><p>You're keen to find performance enhancements and opportunities with the integration of AI into your workflow, whilst retaining creative agency over the output of your work.</p></li></ol><h3>Our Responsibilities</h3><ol><li><p>We will ensure you have meaningful and regular access to your manager to support your personal development and career trajectory.</p></li><li><p>We will actively reward performance with promotions, pay increases and a discretionary annual bonus of up to 10% of salary to all employees.</p></li><li><p>We ensure Koto is an equal environment for all its employees.</p></li><li><p>We will support you in taking family leave and offer significant paid maternity and paternity.</p></li><li><p>We will actively encourage you to take 25 days holiday yearly, in addition to UK bank holidays (on average eight), one additional holiday day per year (capped at 5 additional days), and a studio closure between Christmas and New Year.</p></li><li><p>We will contribute an additional 3% to anyone wanting to engage in the Scottish Widows pension scheme.</p></li><li><p>We offer private medical insurance via Vitality Health and a monthly ‘employee wellness’ benefits package via Juno of £75 per month.</p></li><li><p>We offer access to YCN and fund relevant training opportunities to any employee looking to develop themselves and career.</p></li><li><p>We reward five years of service with £5,000 and five days holiday. We reward ten years of service with £10,000 and four weeks holiday.</p></li><li><p>We will give you the opportunity to work from any the four koto studios globally, with appropriate planning and application.</p></li></ol><h3>Our Culture</h3><ul><li><p>At Koto we encourage our team to be ‘rationally optimistic’ about the potential of our work, our clients, their businesses, and the power of brand.</p></li><li><p>We pursue the best briefs, want to inspire meaningful work, and for those interested provide an opportunity to make the work of their lives. We are actively cultivating leadership, culture and spaces to encourage this.</p></li><li><p>We work in person with an optional WFH day on Friday, and an additional twenty days remote a year. We believe this produces the best work, mentoring opportunities, collective learning and culture.</p></li><li><p>We encourage autonomy in each member of our team, allowing them to manage their own time, hours and personal commitments appropriately to complete work.</p></li></ul>

title: Midweight Designer
description: <p>You are a designer refining your craft. Your days are spent relentlessly authoring ideas, designing identities, collaborating with the team around you.</p><h3>Your Responsibilities</h3><ol><li><p>You generate great ideas and know how to articulate them well both visually and verbally.</p></li><li><p>You spend a significant amount of time designing and have a strong understanding of symbolism, typography, colour, illustration, iconography and art-direction. You can compose all of these components into complete brand identities.</p></li><li><p>You collaborate intimately with the team around you, learning from them and supporting junior staff. You are able to follow direction from the senior team.</p></li><li><p>You can construct components of client presentations and are beginning your journey to present work.</p></li><li><p>You assist with and demonstrate an interest in our immersion and strategic phases of work.</p></li><li><p>You demonstrate an active interest in branding and want to deliver work for the most impactful companies of today and the founders defining tomorrow.</p></li><li><p>You demonstrate ownership of your role and can both take and action feedback from your manager to progress within it and beyond.</p></li><li><p>You are able to manage your calendar, your time and deadlines to finish tasks.</p></li><li><p>You have a willingness to work in person in our London studio and are open to travel globally, from Stuttgart to San Francisco.</p></li><li><p>You have a great understanding of Figma, InDesign, Illustrator, Photoshop, and Pitch.</p></li></ol><h3>Our Responsibilities</h3><ol><li><p>We will ensure you have meaningful and regular access to your manager to support your personal development and career trajectory.</p></li><li><p>We will actively reward performance with promotions, pay increases and a discretionary annual bonus of up to 10% of salary to all employees.</p></li><li><p>We ensure Koto is an equal environment for all its employees.</p></li><li><p>We will support you in taking family leave and offer significant paid maternity and paternity.</p></li><li><p>We will actively encourage you to take 25 days holiday yearly, in addition to UK bank holidays (on average eight), one additional holiday day per year (capped at 5 additional days), and a studio closure between Christmas and New Year.</p></li><li><p>We will contribute an additional 3% to anyone wanting to engage in the Scottish Widows pension scheme.</p></li><li><p>We offer private medical insurance via Vitality Health and a monthly ‘employee wellness’ benefits package via Juno of £75 per month.</p></li><li><p>We offer access to YCN and fund relevant training opportunities to any employee looking to develop themselves and career.</p></li><li><p>We reward five years of service with £5,000 and five days holiday. We reward ten years of service with £10,000 and four weeks holiday.</p></li><li><p>We will give you the opportunity to work from any the four koto studios globally, with appropriate planning and application.</p></li></ol><h3>Our Culture</h3><ul><li><p>At Koto we encourage our team to be 'rationally optimistic<strong>’</strong> about the potential of our work, our clients, their businesses, and the power of brand.</p></li><li><p>We pursue the best briefs, want to inspire meaningful work, and for those interested provide an opportunity to make the work of their lives. We are actively cultivating leadership, culture and spaces to encourage this.</p></li><li><p>We work in person with an optional WFH day on Friday, and an additional twenty days remote a year. We believe this produces the best work, mentoring opportunities, collective learning and culture.</p></li><li><p>We encourage autonomy in each member of our team, allowing them to manage their own time, hours and personal commitments appropriately to complete work.</p></li></ul>

This privacy policy also describes what rights you have when we process your personal data, and how you can exercise these rights.When we use the term “Candidate” in this privacy policy, we are referring to each of Connecting Candidates; Applying Candidates; Sourced Candidates; and Referred Candidates, unless it’s stated otherwise.1. About processing of personal dataPersonal data is all information that can be directly or indirectly linked to a living, physical person. Examples of personal data are: name, e-mail address, telephone number and IP address. Processing of personal data is any automated use of personal data - such as collecting, creating, analyzing, sharing, and deleting personal data.There are laws and regulations on how companies may process personal data, so-called data protection laws. Different data protection laws apply to different types of use of personal data, and in different parts of the world. An example of a data protection law that is relevant for our use of your personal data, as described in this privacy policy, is the EU Data Protection Regulation (2016/679, “GDPR”). Most obligations under the GDPR apply to the so-called data controller. A data controller is the entity that decides for which purposes personal data will be processed, and how the processing will be executed. The data controller can use a so-called data processor. A data processor is an entity that is only allowed to process personal data as instructed by the data controller, and may not use the personal data for its own purposes.We are the data controller when we process your personal data as described in this Privacy policy.2. What personal data do we process?All individuals Device information - If you visit our Career Site, we will collect information about your device, such as IP address, browser type and version, session behaviour, traffic source, screen resolution, preferred language, geographic location, operating system and device settings/usage. Technical and statistical data - If you visit our Career Site, we will collect technical and statistical data about your use of the site, such as information about which URLs you visit, and your activity on the site. Communications data - We will collect and store your communication with us, including the information you provided in the communication. This may include the content of emails, video recordings, messages on social media, the information you add to your account with us, surveys, etc. Contact details - Such as your name, email address, telephone number and physical address. Candidates Data from interviews, assessments and other information from the recruitment process - Such as notes from interviews with you, assessments and tests made, salary requirements. Information in your application - Such as your CV, cover letter, work samples, references, letters of recommendation and education. Information in your public profile - Meaning the information we collect about you from public sources related to your professional experience, such as LinkedIn or the website of your current employer. Information provided by references - Meaning the information we receive from our employees or partners who refer you to us, or by the persons you have listed as your references. 3. Where do we receive your personal data from?All individuals From the Career Site. If you visit our Career Site, we collect technical and statistical information about how you use the Career Site, and information from your device. Directly from you. Most of the information we process about you, we receive directly from you, for example when you apply for a position with us or connect with us. You can always choose not to provide us with certain information. However, some personal data is necessary in order for us to process your application or provide you the information you request to get from us. References From the person for whom you are a reference. If a Candidate lists you as their reference, we will collect your contact details from the candidate to be able to contact you. We may receive information about you from our employees or partners (such as recruitment service providers), when they believe your profile is of interest for our current or future vacancies. We use the following safeguards: A decision by the EU Commission that the country outside of the EU/EEA to which your personal data is transferred has an adequate level of protection, which corresponds to the level of protection afforded by the GDPR. In particular, we rely on the EU Commission’s adequacy decision for the US via the so-called EU-US Data Privacy Framework, and the adequacy decision for the UK. Entering into the EU Commission’s standard clauses with the recipient of the personal data outside the EU/EEA. This means that the recipient guarantees that the level of protection for your personal data afforded by the GDPR still applies, and that your rights are still protected. When your personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA, we also implement appropriate technical and organizational safeguards, to protect the personal data in case of a disclosure. Exactly which protective measures we implement depends on what is technically feasible, and sufficiently effective, for the particular transfer.If you want more information about the cases in which your personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA you can contact us using the contact details in Section 9 and 10 below.8. For how long do we keep your personal data?All individualsIf we process your personal data for the purpose of being able to protect and enforce our rights, we will keep your personal data until the relevant legal issue has been fully and finally resolved. title: Senior Designer
description: <h3>The Role</h3><p>You are an experienced designer, your days are spent relentlessly authoring ideas, designing identities and inspiring an exceptional quality of work in the team around you.</p><h3>Your Responsibilities</h3><ol><li><p>You generate excellent ideas and know how to articulate them expertly both visually and verbally. You run creative catch-ups and idea generating sessions to actively increase the quality of thought in your team.</p></li><li><p>You spend a significant amount of time designing, have a strong understanding of, and can create briefs for: symbolism, typography, colour, illustration, iconography and art-direction. You can compose all of these components into complete world-class brand identities, inspire and lead others to the same result, and practically support their design where needed.</p></li><li><p>You collaborate intimately with the team around you, learning from them and supporting junior staff. You are able to follow direction from the senior team.</p></li><li><p>You can construct client presentations and can present work eloquently.</p></li><li><p>You assist with and can translate our immersion and strategic phases of work into design execution.</p></li><li><p>You demonstrate a deep knowledge of branding and the creative practitioners within it. You consistently deliver work for the most impactful companies of today and the founders defining tomorrow.</p></li><li><p>You demonstrate ownership of your role and can both take and action feedback from your manager to progress within it and beyond.</p></li><li><p>You have a strong awareness of the time required to complete design tasks. You are able to successfully manage your own time, calendar and meetings.</p></li><li><p>You have a willingness to work in person in our London studio and are open to travel globally, from Stuttgart to San Francisco.</p></li><li><p>You have a great understanding of Figma, InDesign, Illustrator, Photoshop, and Pitch.You spend a significant amount of time designing, have a strong understanding of, and can create briefs for: symbolism, typography, colour, illustration, iconography and art-direction. You can compose all of these components into complete world-class brand identities, inspire and lead others to the same result, and practically support their design where needed.</p></li><li><p>You collaborate intimately with the team around you, learning from them and supporting junior staff. You are able to follow direction from the senior team.</p></li><li><p>You can construct client presentations and can present work eloquently.</p></li><li><p>You assist with and can translate our immersion and strategic phases of work into design execution.</p></li><li><p>You demonstrate a deep knowledge of branding and the creative practitioners within it. You consistently deliver work for the most impactful companies of today and the founders defining tomorrow.</p></li><li><p>You demonstrate ownership of your role and can both take and action feedback from your manager to progress within it and beyond.</p></li><li><p>You have a strong awareness of the time required to complete design tasks. You are able to successfully manage your own time, calendar and meetings.</p></li><li><p>You have a willingness to work in person in our London studio and are open to travel globally, from Stuttgart to San Francisco.</p></li><li><p>You have a great understanding of Figma, InDesign, Illustrator, Photoshop, and Pitch.</p></li></ol><h3>Our Responsibilities</h3><ol><li><p>We will ensure you have meaningful and regular access to your manager to support your personal development and career trajectory.</p></li><li><p>We will actively reward performance with promotions, pay increases and a discretionary annual bonus of up to 10% of salary to all employees.</p></li><li><p>We ensure Koto is an equal environment for all its employees.</p></li><li><p>We will support you in taking family leave and offer significant paid maternity and paternity.</p></li><li><p>We will actively encourage you to take 25 days holiday yearly, in addition to UK bank holidays (on average eight), one additional holiday day per year (capped at 5 additional days), and a studio closure between Christmas and New Year.</p></li><li><p>We will contribute an additional 3% to anyone wanting to engage in the Scottish Widows pension scheme.</p></li><li><p>We offer private medical insurance via Vitality Health and a monthly ‘employee wellness’ benefits package via Juno of £75 per month.</p></li><li><p>We offer access to YCN and fund relevant training opportunities to any employee looking to develop themselves and career.</p></li><li><p>We reward five years of service with £5,000 and five days holiday. We reward ten years of service with £10,000 and four weeks holiday.</p></li><li><p>We will give you the opportunity to work from any the four koto studios globally, with appropriate planning and application.</p></li></ol><h3>Our Culture</h3><ul><li><p>At Koto we encourage our team to be ‘rationally optimistic’ about the potential of our work, our clients, their businesses, and the power of brand.</p></li><li><p>We pursue the best briefs, want to inspire meaningful work, and for those interested provide an opportunity to make the work of their lives. We are actively cultivating leadership, culture and spaces to encourage this.</p></li><li><p>We work in person with an optional WFH day on Friday, and an additional twenty days remote a year. We believe this produces the best work, mentoring opportunities, collective learning and culture.</p></li><li><p>We encourage autonomy in each member of our team, allowing them to manage their own time, hours and personal commitments appropriately to complete work.</p></li></ul>

title: Design Director
description: <h3>The Role</h3><p>You are equal parts designer and leader — a true tastemaker with strong creative judgment and a clear point of view. This is a deeply embedded role, dedicated to a single, complex, future-facing platform where creativity, systems thinking, and generative tools intersect. You understand both creativity and practicality, and you navigate that duality with confidence. You inspire and do. You manage up and down. You bring clarity to complexity and momentum to ideas, delivering exceptional brand work with rigor, taste, and intention.</p><h3>Your Responsibilities</h3><ol><li><p>You identify, curate, and refine standout ideas in close collaboration with the Creative Director. You bring sharp, creative judgment and a refined point of view, framing work clearly and persuasively. You encourage and nurture this same level of taste and discernment within your team.</p></li><li><p>You split your time between leading and designing. You champion exceptional brand identities and are hands-on in making them real — designing directly when needed and directing photographers, animators, illustrators, and specialist craft collaborators to push the work in new directions.</p></li><li><p>You bring a strong, informed perspective on generative tools and AI. You understand the broader AI landscape and can engage in thoughtful, critical discourse — shaping strong prompts and systems, challenging ideas constructively, and ensuring the work is innovative, intentional, and never driven by hype.</p></li><li><p>You collaborate deeply with the team around you, mentoring wherever possible. You manage both up and down, acting as a trusted conduit between the Creative Director and Designers, ensuring clarity, momentum, and creative excellence.</p></li><li><p>You build compelling narratives, deliver thoughtful presentations, and confidently lead workshops and client working sessions. You don’t just present work — you extend the dialogue, challenge assumptions, and evolve ideas in true partnership.</p></li><li><p>You collaborate closely with the Strategy team throughout immersion and strategic phases, shaping creative responses that are conceptually strong, culturally relevant, and commercially sound.</p></li><li><p>You demonstrate an exceptional understanding of branding and the creative practitioners within it. You consistently deliver work for today’s most impactful companies and the builders shaping what comes next.</p></li><li><p>You demonstrate ownership of your role and actively seek, take, and apply feedback from your manager. You provide clear, actionable feedback and meaningful support to your team, helping them grow creatively and professionally.</p></li><li><p>You have a strong awareness of the time, craft, and collaboration required to produce excellent work. You manage your own time and your team’s time effectively — calendars, meetings, and scopes — to deliver high-quality work on schedule.</p></li><li><p>You are comfortable working in person from our Culver City studio and are open to global travel when needed.</p></li><li><p>You have a strong command of Figma, InDesign, Illustrator, Photoshop, and Pitch, and stay curious about emerging tools and workflows that elevate the work.</p></li></ol><h3>Our Responsibilities</h3><ol><li><p>We will ensure you have meaningful and regular access to your manager to support your personal development and career trajectory.</p></li><li><p>We will actively reward performance with promotions, pay increases, and a discretionary annual bonus of up to 10% of salary to all employees.</p></li><li><p>We ensure Koto is an equal environment for all its employees.</p></li><li><p>We will support you in taking family leave and offer significant paid maternity and paternity.</p></li><li><p>We will actively encourage you to take 25 vacation days per year, in addition to US national holidays (on average 10) and a studio closure between Christmas and New Year.</p></li><li><p>We offer a 401(k) plan you can contribute to, and Koto matches up to 4%.</p></li><li><p>We deploy a monthly ‘employee wellness’ benefits package via Juno of $105 per month.</p></li><li><p>We offer YCN access and fund relevant training opportunities for any employee looking to develop themselves and their career.</p></li><li><p>We reward five years of service with $5,000 and five days' holiday. We reward ten years of service with $10,000 and four weeks' holiday.</p></li><li><p>We will give you the opportunity to work from any of the five Koto studios globally, with appropriate planning and application.</p></li></ol><h3>Our Culture</h3><ul><li><p>At Koto, we encourage our team to be ‘rationally optimistic’ about the potential of our work, our clients, their businesses, and the power of brand.</p></li><li><p>We pursue the best briefs, want to inspire meaningful work, and, for those interested, provide an opportunity to make the work of their lives. We are actively cultivating leadership, culture, and spaces to encourage this.</p></li><li><p>We work in person with an optional WFH day on Friday and an additional 2 weeks of remote work per year. We believe this produces the best work, mentoring opportunities, collective learning, and culture.</p></li><li><p>We encourage autonomy among our team members, allowing them to manage their own time, hours, and personal commitments to complete their work.</p></li></ul>
