Privacy policyDate of publication: 07-06-2024We at Koto manage our employer branding and recruitment process through our career site (the “Career Site”), and by using a related applicant tracking system.In this privacy policy, we explain how we process your personal data if: You visit our Career Site (you being a “Visitor”) You connect with us via our Career Site, to create a profile with us and receive information about current or future vacancies with us (you being a “Connecting Candidate”) You apply for a position with us, via our Career Site or a third party service (you being an ”Applying Candidate”) We collect information about you from other parties, sites and services, since we believe your profile is of interest for our current or future vacancies (you being a “Sourced Candidate”) We receive information about you from our employees or partners, since they believe your profile is of interest for our current or future vacancies (you being a “Referred Candidate”) We receive information about you from a Candidate, who lists you as their reference (you being a “Reference”). This privacy policy also describes what rights you have when we process your personal data, and how you can exercise these rights.When we use the term “Candidate” in this privacy policy, we are referring to each of Connecting Candidates; Applying Candidates; Sourced Candidates; and Referred Candidates, unless it’s stated otherwise.1. About processing of personal dataPersonal data is all information that can be directly or indirectly linked to a living, physical person. Examples of personal data are: name, e-mail address, telephone number and IP address. Processing of personal data is any automated use of personal data - such as collecting, creating, analyzing, sharing, and deleting personal data.There are laws and regulations on how companies may process personal data, so-called data protection laws. Different data protection laws apply to different types of use of personal data, and in different parts of the world. An example of a data protection law that is relevant for our use of your personal data, as described in this privacy policy, is the EU Data Protection Regulation (2016/679, “GDPR”). Most obligations under the GDPR apply to the so-called data controller. A data controller is the entity that decides for which purposes personal data will be processed, and how the processing will be executed. The data controller can use a so-called data processor. A data processor is an entity that is only allowed to process personal data as instructed by the data controller, and may not use the personal data for its own purposes.We are the data controller when we process your personal data as described in this Privacy policy.2. What personal data do we process?All individuals Device information - If you visit our Career Site, we will collect information about your device, such as IP address, browser type and version, session behaviour, traffic source, screen resolution, preferred language, geographic location, operating system and device settings/usage. Technical and statistical data - If you visit our Career Site, we will collect technical and statistical data about your use of the site, such as information about which URLs you visit, and your activity on the site. Communications data - We will collect and store your communication with us, including the information you provided in the communication. This may include the content of emails, video recordings, messages on social media, the information you add to your account with us, surveys, etc. Contact details - Such as your name, email address, telephone number and physical address. Candidates Data from interviews, assessments and other information from the recruitment process - Such as notes from interviews with you, assessments and tests made, salary requirements. Information in your application - Such as your CV, cover letter, work samples, references, letters of recommendation and education. Information in your public profile - Meaning the information we collect about you from public sources related to your professional experience, such as LinkedIn or the website of your current employer. Information provided by references - Meaning the information we receive from our employees or partners who refer you to us, or by the persons you have listed as your references. 3. Where do we receive your personal data from?All individuals From the Career Site. If you visit our Career Site, we collect technical and statistical information about how you use the Career Site, and information from your device. Directly from you. Most of the information we process about you, we receive directly from you, for example when you apply for a position with us or connect with us. You can always choose not to provide us with certain information. However, some personal data is necessary in order for us to process your application or provide you the information you request to get from us. References From the person for whom you are a reference. If a Candidate lists you as their reference, we will collect your contact details from the candidate to be able to contact you. Candidates From public sources. We may collect personal data about you from public sources, such as LinkedIn or the website of your current employer. From our references. We may receive information about you from our employees or partners (such as recruitment service providers), when they believe your profile is of interest for our current or future vacancies. From your references. If you provide us with references, we may collect information about you from them. Data we create ourselves or in cooperation with you. Information about your application and profile is usually created by us, or by us in cooperation with you, during the recruitment process. This may for example include notes from interviews with you, assessments and tests made. 4. For what purposes do we process your personal data?Protect and enforce our rights, interests and the interests of others, for example in connection with legal claims.Affected individuals: The individual(s) affected by the legal issue - this may include persons from all categories of individuals listed above.Categories of personal data used: All the categories of personal data listed above can be used for this purpose.Share your personal data with other recipients, for the purposes mentioned in Section 5 below.Affected individuals: Varies depending on the purpose of the sharing, see Section 5 below.Categories of personal data used: All the categories of personal data listed above may be used for this purpose.Collect information about your use of the Career Site, using cookies and other tracking technologies, as described in our Cookie Policy.Affected individuals: Visitors.Categories of personal data used: Device information.Maintain, develop, test, and otherwise ensure the security of the Career Site.Affected individuals: Visitors.Categories of personal data used: Device information; Technical and statistical data.Analyse how the Career Site and its content is being used and is performing, to get statistics and to improve operational performance.Affected individuals: Visitors.Categories of personal data used: Device information; Technical and statistical data.Provide you with updates about vacancies with us.Affected individuals: Connecting Candidates. Categories of personal data used: Contact details; Communications data.Review profiles and applications sent to us. This also includes communicating with you about your application and profile. Affected individuals: Connecting Candidates; Applying Candidates. Categories of personal data used: All the categories of personal data listed above may be used for this purpose.Collect and evaluate your professional profile on our own initiative. This also includes communicating with you regarding your profile.Affected individuals: Sourced Candidates; Referred Candidates.Categories of personal data used: All the categories of personal data listed above may be used for this purpose.Contact you directly about specific, future vacancies with us.Affected individuals: Candidates. Categories of personal data used: All the categories of personal data listed above may be used for this purpose.Record the interview(s) with you. Affected individuals: Candidates. Categories of personal data used: Communications data. Contact you to ask for your participation in surveysAffected individuals: Candidates. </p Categories of personal data used: All the categories of personal data listed above may be used for this purpose.Contact you to ask you to provide information about a Candidate, and evaluate the information you provide. Affected individuals: References.Categories of personal data used: Contact details; Communications data. 5. Whom do we share your personal data with?Our service providers. We share your personal data with our suppliers who provide services and functionality in our employer branding- and recruitment process. For example, this includes recruitment service providers and the supplier of our Career Site and related applicant tracking system. Our group companies. We share your personal data with our group companies, when they provide us services and functionality to our employer branding- and recruitment process, such as access to particular systems and software. Companies providing cookies on the Career Site. If you consent to it, cookies are set by other companies than us, who will use the data collected by these cookies in accordance with their own privacy policy. You can find information about which cookies this applies to in our Cookie Policy. To authorities and other public actors - when we are ordered to do so. We will share your personal data with authorities and other public actors when we have a legal obligation to do so. To parties involved in legal proceedings. If needed to protect or defend our rights, we share your personal data with public authorities or with other parties involved in a potential or existing legal proceeding. This can for example be in case of discrimination claims.Mergers and acquisitions etc. In connection with a potential merger, sale of company assets, financing, or acquisition of all or part of our business to another company, we may share your personal data to other parties involved in the process. 6. On what legal bases do we process your personal data?To be able to process your personal data, we need to have a so-called legal basis. A legal basis is a reason for processing the personal data that is justified under the GDPR.When we process your personal data for the purposes described in this Privacy Policy, the legal basis we rely on is normally that the processing is necessary for our legitimate interest in being able to recruit talent with the relevant competence for us. We have concluded that we have a legitimate interest in being able to perform the personal data processing for this purpose; that the processing is necessary to achieve that purpose; and that our interest outweighs your right not to have your data processed for this purpose. You can contact us for more information about how this assessment was made. See Section 9 and 10 below for our contact information.There may be specific circumstances when the processing is only performed if and when you provide your consent to the processing. This is for example the case if we propose to record an interview with you. Please see Section 9 below for more information about your right to withdraw your consent. 7. When do we transfer your personal data outside of the EU/EEA, and how do we protect it then?We always strive to process your personal data within the EU/EEA area. However, some of our service providers process your personal data outside of the EU/EEA. We also use suppliers whose parent company, or whose subcontractor’s parent company, is based outside the EU/EEA. In these cases, we have taken into account the risk that the personal data may be disclosed to countries outside the EU/EEA, for example because of an authority request.In cases where another recipient of your personal data (as described in Section 5 above) is based outside the EU/EEA, this will also mean that your personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA. When we, or one of our suppliers, transfer your personal data outside the EU/EEA, we will ensure that a safeguard recognized by the GDPR is used to enable the transfer. We use the following safeguards: A decision by the EU Commission that the country outside of the EU/EEA to which your personal data is transferred has an adequate level of protection, which corresponds to the level of protection afforded by the GDPR. In particular, we rely on the EU Commission’s adequacy decision for the US via the so-called EU-US Data Privacy Framework, and the adequacy decision for the UK. Entering into the EU Commission’s standard clauses with the recipient of the personal data outside the EU/EEA. This means that the recipient guarantees that the level of protection for your personal data afforded by the GDPR still applies, and that your rights are still protected. When your personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA, we also implement appropriate technical and organizational safeguards, to protect the personal data in case of a disclosure. Exactly which protective measures we implement depends on what is technically feasible, and sufficiently effective, for the particular transfer.If you want more information about the cases in which your personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA you can contact us using the contact details in Section 9 and 10 below.8. For how long do we keep your personal data?All individualsIf we process your personal data for the purpose of being able to protect and enforce our rights, we will keep your personal data until the relevant legal issue has been fully and finally resolved. VisitorsWe keep your personal data for one (1) year for security purposes. The retention periods for cookies are set out in our Cookie Policy. We keep your personal data to analyse the performance of the Career Site for as long as we keep personal data about you for other purposes. CandidatesIf you are a Connecting Candidate (only), we

title: Design Engineer
description: <h3>The Role<br></h3><p>You are a creative coder, an inventor, and a technical artist. You are the bridge between ambitious creative ideas and tangible, interactive execution. You will sit at the heart of the Design Team, working closely with creative directors and designers to elevate brand and digital projects by applying technology - be it raw code, generative AI, or hacked hardware - in novel and inspiring ways. You look for software solutions to design challenges, so your primary craft is code, not Figma. You build the prototypes, experiences and tools that bring our most imaginative concepts to life. You are driven by creative curiosity, thrive on collaboration, and are deeply tuned into the currents of technology, brands, and contemporary culture.</p><h3>Your Responsibilities</h3><ol><li><p><strong>You translate creative and strategic concepts into functional prototypes and interactive experiences</strong>. Your primary output is code — whether it’s a web-based tool for generating patterns, a real-time 3D visualisation, or a prompt-driven AI tool that empowers our designers to work smarter and faster.</p></li><li><p><strong>You collaborate closely with design and creative teams from the very beginning.</strong> You bring technical insight and creative experimentation into early concept development, helping shape, challenge, and expand the core idea.</p></li><li><p><strong>You operate as the studio’s R&amp;D engine.</strong> You proactively explore, test, and master emerging technologies — from generative AI and creative coding frameworks to AR/VR and physical computing — and share your discoveries to elevate and inspire the wider team.</p></li><li><p><strong>You write inventive, expressive, AND robust code across the entire spectrum of prototyping and production.</strong> You move fluidly from fast, AI-assisted “vibe-coding” for quick conceptual sketches to mature, production-ready frameworks like React and Next.js when structure and stability are required. You default to rapid creative exploration but know when rigour matters.</p></li><li><p><strong>You embrace and help pioneer new tools, workflows, and platforms</strong>. From established web technologies to collaborating with advanced AI models or building with more esoteric environments, you continually expand the studio’s creative and technical capabilities.</p></li><li><p><strong>You understand how technology deepens the relationship between brands and humans. </strong>You have an instinct for how interactive systems, tools, and digital moments can create ownable, memorable, and emotionally resonant brand experiences.</p></li><li><p><strong>You communicate complex technical ideas with clarity and enthusiasm.</strong> You articulate your thinking to both technical and non-technical collaborators and clients, helping them understand — and get excited about — the potential of your work.</p></li><li><p><strong>You contribute to our culture in person.</strong> You’re willing to work from our London studio, participate in our collaborative environment, and travel globally for project needs — from Stuttgart to San Francisco.</p></li></ol><ol><li><p></p></li></ol><h3><strong>Our Responsibilities</strong></h3><ol><li><p>We will ensure you have meaningful and regular access to your manager to support your personal development and career trajectory.</p></li><li><p>We will actively reward performance with promotions, pay increases and a discretionary annual bonus of up to 10% of salary to all employees.</p></li><li><p>We ensure Koto is an equal environment for all its employees regardless of race, gender, age, or sexual preference.</p></li><li><p>We will support you in taking family leave and offer significant paid maternity and paternity.</p></li><li><p>We will actively encourage you to take 25 days holiday yearly, in addition to UK bank holidays (on average eight), one additional holiday day per year (capped at 5 additional days), and a studio closure between Christmas and New Year.</p></li><li><p>We will contribute an additional 3% to anyone wanting to engage in the People's Pension Scheme following their 3m probation.</p></li><li><p>We deploy a monthly 'employee wellness' benefits package via Juno of £75 per month.</p></li><li><p>We offer access to YCN and fund relevant training opportunities to any employee looking to develop themselves and career.</p></li><li><p>We reward five years of service with £5,000 and five days holiday. We reward ten years of service with £10,000 and four weeks holiday.</p></li><li><p>We will give you the opportunity to work from any of the four koto studios globally, with appropriate planning and application.</p></li></ol><ol><li><p></p></li></ol><h3><br></h3><h3>Our Culture</h3><ul><li><p>At Koto we encourage our team to be ‘rationally optimistic’ about the potential of our work, our clients, their businesses, and the power of brand.</p></li><li><p>We pursue the best briefs, want to inspire meaningful work, and for those interested provide an opportunity to make the work of their lives. We are actively cultivating leadership, culture and spaces to encourage this.</p></li><li><p>We work in person with an optional WFH day on Friday, and an additional twenty days remote a year. We believe this produces the best work, mentoring opportunities, collective learning and culture.</p></li><li><p>We encourage autonomy in each member of our team, allowing them to manage their own time, hours and personal commitments appropriately to complete work.</p></li></ul><p><strong>#LI-DNI</strong></p>

In these cases, we have taken into account the risk that the personal data may be disclosed to countries outside the EU/EEA, for example because of an authority request.In cases where another recipient of your personal data (as described in Section 5 above) is based outside the EU/EEA, this will also mean that your personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA. When we, or one of our suppliers, transfer your personal data outside the EU/EEA, we will ensure that a safeguard recognized by the GDPR is used to enable the transfer. We use the following safeguards: A decision by the EU Commission that the country outside of the EU/EEA to which your personal data is transferred has an adequate level of protection, which corresponds to the level of protection afforded by the GDPR. In particular, we rely on the EU Commission’s adequacy decision for the US via the so-called EU-US Data Privacy Framework, and the adequacy decision for the UK. Entering into the EU Commission’s standard clauses with the recipient of the personal data outside the EU/EEA. This means that the recipient guarantees that the level of protection for your personal data afforded by the GDPR still applies, and that your rights are still protected. When your personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA, we also implement appropriate technical and organizational safeguards, to protect the personal data in case of a disclosure. Exactly which protective measures we implement depends on what is technically feasible, and sufficiently effective, for the particular transfer.If you want more information about the cases in which your personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA you can contact us using the contact details in Section 9 and 10 below.8. description: [back] Cosmonauts Brand Catalogue Art Direction, Editorial Design The Cosmonauts Brand Catalogue showcases an exceptional collection of high-performance sports sunglasses inspired by the infinite possibilities of space exploration. Each pair embodies a perfect union of advanced technology and futuristic aesthetics, offering athletes unparalleled style, durability, and functionality. Beyond its visual appeal, the design emphasizes clarity and usability, ensuring that every detail serves a purpose. The dynamic interplay of form and function creates a forward-thinking visual narrative that celebrates both the brand’s commitment to excellence and the adventurous spirit of its audience. The catalogue stands as a compelling representation of futuristic vision brought to life. title: Copywriter - (m/f/d) MILAN
description: <p><strong>ABOUT THE JOB </strong></p><p>As a Copywriter, you will play a key role in shaping ideas from initial insight through to final delivery. You will contribute to both local and international projects across a diverse client portfolio, crafting distinctive brand voices and selecting the most effective channels to bring each concept to life. Working closely with an Art Director, you will also collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to ensure every project is executed with creativity, consistency, and impact.</p><p></p><p><strong>ABOUT YOU</strong></p><ul><li><p>At least 3 years of experience in a similar role</p></li><li><p>Proven ability to develop integrated 360° communication projects (ATL, BTL, digital, social), in line with strategic guidelines and briefs</p></li><li><p>Strong knowledge of major social platforms, including <span>Facebook</span>, <span>Instagram</span>, <span>X</span>, <span>LinkedIn</span>, <span>YouTube</span>, and <span>TikTok</span>, with up-to-date knowledge of trends and best practices</p></li><li><p>Curious mindset with a passion for exploring new formats and creative languages</p></li><li><p>Out-of-the-box thinker with strong conceptual skills</p></li><li><p>Excellent English proficiency (C1 level or equivalent)</p></li><li><p>Nice to have: interest in the automotive and/or energy sectors</p></li></ul><p></p><p><span>Applications close <strong>Monday, March 16, 2026. </strong></span></p><p></p><p><strong>ABOUT M+C SAATCHI GROUP</strong></p><div><p>We are a creative solutions company deeply rooted in the diversity of European culture. We think globally, operate with a Pan-European mindset and act locally. Our team is our greatest strength, fostering a culture of collaboration, creativity and impact. We prioritise ideas and are obsessed with production, crafting culture-led communication that transcends channels, ignites people’s passions, sparks meaningful connections around social issues and creates unforgettable stories that resonate across multiple touchpoints. By blending strategic thinking with creativity, we assemble teams exactly for the task at hand, driving maximum impact for our clients.</p></div><div><p>We are the world’s largest independent creative network, across 6 regions and 22 countries. We connect specialist expertise — powered by creativity, data and technology — to help build famous brands, solve the most complex business problems, and tackle society’s biggest challenges.</p></div><div><p>M+C Saatchi Group was founded in 1995 and is now the biggest Independent creative agency group in the World. Founded on one core principle, Brutal Simplicity.</p></div>

title: Graphic Designer
description: <p><strong style="border:0px solid rgb(238, 239, 242);font-weight:600;color:inherit;">About Us:</strong></p><p>M+C Saatchi Performance is a global digital media agency, connecting brands to people. We deliver business growth for our clients through effective, measurable, and evolving digital media strategies.</p><p>What are we looking for?</p><p><span>Inquisitive, creative and your default is to find solutions by making. Hands-on experience in digital media production means you can concept, design, produce and put the finishing touches on any story. You are as obsessed with culture as you are with content deeply connected to social media, current events, the zeitgeist, and trends. You know how to make content that get clicks!</span></p><p><strong>The Role</strong></p><p>-Produce design for a variety of uses and find the sweet spot that lives at the intersection of quality creative and resourcefulness.</p><p>-Work alongside a team of designers &amp; copywriter to create and conceptualise designs for creative campaigns, social media, and marketing briefs.</p><p>-Creating and/or overlooking and reviewing the production assets that come in from the external/internal production team.</p><p>-Develop thoughtful creative work that delivers on the overall strategy within the required turnaround times.</p><p><strong><br>Qualifications</strong></p><p>- 2 to 3 years of experience with creating designs, concepts, and sample layouts based on knowledge of layout principles and aesthetic design concepts. (Great if you have a prior advertising/digital/social media agency background)</p><p>- Expertise in Adobe Photoshop and Adobe Illustrator is a must. After Effects would be a great add-on (or you should be willing to learn). Other software skills like Figma, Canva, use of Ai tools would be a great add on.</p><p>-Ability to be collaborative and flexible in working with diverse teams and with shifting priorities.</p><p>-Experience working with Copywriters, Social Media Strategists and Account Managers.</p><p>-Fluent professional level English.</p><p>-Keep up with online social media trends and design in the digital space.</p><p>What you can expect from working at M+C Saatchi Performance</p><p>-A vast insight into the world of digital marketing and in-depth knowledge across search and social advertising.</p><p>-Experience working on global projects across client verticals.</p><p>-Experience working on digital advertising campaigns for large global brands.</p><p>-Experience in working with a team in an agency environment.</p><p>-Insight into the M+C Saatchi Group.</p><p>M+C Saatchi Performance as part of M+C Saatchi Group has pledged its commitment to create a company that values difference, with an inclusive culture. As part of this, M+C Saatchi Group continues to be an Equal Opportunity Employer which does not and shall not discriminate, celebrates diversity and bases all hiring and promotion decisions solely on merit, without regard for any personal characteristics.<br><br>All employee information is kept confidential according to General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).</p>

title: Growth Bid Writer
description: <hr><hr><p><span>We are currently looking for a Growth Bid Writer to join the new business team in one of the groups specialist divisions. This role sits within M&amp;C Saatchi World Services of M&amp;C Saatchi Group. World Services is a specialist division of M&amp;C Saatchi Group. Partnering with governments, civil society, foundations, academia, and the private sector, we work on the critical issues of our time, including national security, social justice and conflict prevention.</span></p><p><strong>ABOUT THE ROLE:</strong></p><p>As a Growth Bid Writer, you will lead the development of compelling, compliant, and market‑specific proposals, ranging from expressions of interest to full commercial tender submissions, ensuring they effectively meet and exceed client requirements. This includes analysing tender documentation, translating complex delivery approaches into clear and persuasive copy, and collaborating with internal experts and market leads across global offices.</p><p><strong>What you'll do:</strong></p><ul><li><p><span>Focus on bid procurement (writing) for UK government clients and/or EU or European country clients.</span></p></li><li><p><span>Writing and editorial oversight on commercial tender submissions, as well as developing proposals for expressions of interest.</span></p></li><li><p><span>Analysing and assessing commercial tender requirements and developing copy that successfully scores maximum points available on those requirements.</span></p></li><li><p><span>Developing new business proposals that are market specific and compliant as well as compelling.</span></p></li><li><p><span>Attending market engagement events to engage on potential tender requirements.</span></p></li><li><p><span>Liaising and engaging market-based business leads to develop persuasive reports and presentations for generating business opportunities.</span></p></li><li><p><span>Engaging with in-house experts to translate oft-complex delivery ideas and mechanisms into highly readable and understandable content.</span></p></li></ul><p><strong>ABOUT YOU</strong></p><p>The successful candidate will bring bid writing experience, producing clear, persuasive, and compliant proposals for public‑sector or international clients. They will be an exceptional written communicator with strong attention to detail, able to translate complex ideas into accessible content while managing multiple deadlines in a fast‑paced environment. Fluent in English with excellent proofreading skills, they will work effectively both independently and collaboratively, with additional European languages, EMEA bid‑winning experience, or knowledge and the opportunity to participate in several active Employee Led Networks and associated events. Finally, this role will be supported with all the necessary personal development required to set someone up for success.</p><p></p><p><strong>ABOUT M+C SAATCHI WORLD SERVICES</strong></p><p><span>M+C Saatchi World Services is a specialist division of M+C Saatchi Group, the world’s largest independent communications network. Our specialists work in office hubs across six continents, grounding our global expertise in local nuance.</span></p><p><span>We offer award-winning commercial communications capabilities, which are utilized by global organizations to address the world’s most complex problems across defence, development, diplomacy, homeland and national security, law and order, health, education, and the environment.</span></p><p><span>We work with clients that want to help the hardest-to-reach, hardest-to-influence audiences. This includes a range of Western Government departments, such as DOS and USAID; partner governments, including the U.K. and Australia; and prominent IGOs/NGOs such as the U.N.</span></p><p><span>We are a comms agency that works on behaviour change projects typically focused on issues related to Security, Defence and Stabilization. Whether it’s producing critically acclaimed feature films in Africa, to TV shows in the Middle East, to pop music videos in Asia, we have a record of leveraging creativity to access communities anywhere in the world.</span></p><p>#WS #LI-BM1</p>

title: Motion Designer
description: Our StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsMenuOur StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsHomeOpen positionsVacancyMotion DesignerDepartment: MarketingLocations: London, New YorkJob Requisition ID: JR001588Apply nowAbout the Team The Brand team at Miro is responsible for how the company shows up in the world: from campaigns and product launches to events and social channels. We're a small, collaborative group of creatives who work across the full spectrum of brand expression, partnering closely with product marketing, corporate marketing, and events. We move fast, care deeply about craft, and are increasingly leaning into AI tools to push what's possible. About the Role We're looking for an ambitious, well-rounded Motion Designer to join our Brand team. This person understands that motion design is storytelling - you're not just animating, you're communicating ideas and driving action. You have a point of view on what makes advertising work and how motion can elevate a brand. The right person has great taste, strong 2D animation fundamentals, and can work across the full production process: from storyboarding through editing and sound. You're comfortable working fast and producing at volume. And you're curious about AI content generation tools - not necessarily an expert, but eager to experiment and learn. We're building a campaign right now that's produced entirely with AI, and we need someone who wants to be part of that. What you’ll do Create motion content for product launches, brand campaigns, social, and events like Canvas • Own projects end-to-end: concept, storyboard, animation, editing, and sound design • Translate complex product features into clear, engaging visual stories • Collaborate with product marketing, corporate marketing, and events teams • Experiment with AI image and video tools to create content at scale • Help evolve Miro's motion design language and maintain consistency across touchpoints What you’ll need 5+ years of professional motion design experience Exceptional 2D animation skills with a portfolio that demonstrates strong taste and timing Experience across the full production cycle - storyboarding, animation, editing, sound Comfort working fast and managing multiple projects with competing deadlines Familiarity with AI image/video generation tools (Runway especially) and eagerness to learn more Self-direction: you take a brief and run with it without heavy oversight What's in it for you We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. description: Our StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsMenuOur StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsHomeOpen positionsVacancySenior Content DesignerDepartment: DesignLocations: LondonJob Requisition ID: JR001613Apply nowAbout the Team We have a small but mighty content design team at Miro, with just three people across multiple key initiatives. This means we must be flexible, resilient and good at prioritising projects where we can have the biggest impact! We're an integral part of the Design org, shaping the user experience for some of our most impactful initiatives. We operate within a hybrid model, embedding deeply within high-priority cross-functional teams to craft user-centered content that guides and delights our users, and offering light support to lower priority work. About the Role We’re looking for a talented Content Designer and AI whizz to join our team and contribute directly to high-impact projects that elevate the Miro experience. This role will sit within our AI Canvas team, specifically working on enhancing our AI Workflow experiences. You'll partner with senior leadership across product, design, and engineering, and build the frameworks that help other teams create consistent AI content at scale. You’ll also be a key player in shaping the content design discipline at Miro, collaborating closely with product managers, designers, researchers, and product marketers and delivering content that enhances usability and drives adoption. This is an exciting opportunity to make a meaningful impact on our product and be part of growing the maturity of our content design practice. What you’ll do Work with product, UX, and engineering teams to help shape the product vision and create user-focused content for high-priority AI features and initiatives – ensuring clarity and consistency across the product Design and develop prompts, UI copy and error messages to help our users adopt Sidekicks and Flows and get high quality results when using them Plan, develop and deliver conversational content that guides users effectively, reduces cognitive load, and aligns with Miro’s voice and tone guidelines Contribute to the refinement and application of Miro’s content design guidelines, particularly focused on refining our AI principles and guidance which will ultimately need to live in our design system and MCPs Collaborate with design and UX research to conduct user research, test content hypotheses, and iterate solutions based on data and feedback Participate in design and product reviews, providing clear rationale for your content design decisions Advocate for the value of content design, helping to integrate the discipline into the broader product development process On a typical day you'll be: Writing and testing system prompts, then debugging the model Defining evaluation criteria with engineering and reviewing AI outputs against quality benchmarks Shaping conversation flows and interaction patterns in design crits and tiger teams Running through edge cases to figure out where the AI breaks and how to recover gracefully Creating conversation starters and prompt suggestions that teach users what's actually possible What you’ll need 6+ years of experience in content design or UX writing, with a strong background in creating content for digital products A strong portfolio showcasing your ability to create clear, user-focused content and demonstrating the impact on user experience and business goals Proven experience working closely with cross-functional teams, including product management, UX design, and engineering, to deliver seamless content solutions Deep understanding of UX writing principles, information architecture, and user-centered design methodologies Familiarity with AI products, conversation design, or prompt engineering (you don't need to be an engineer, but you should be comfortable getting hands-on with how AI systems behave) Ability to align product content strategy with business objectives, balancing user needs with technical feasibility Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate the value of content design and collaborate effectively across diverse teams A proactive and flexible approach, comfortable working autonomously in a fast-paced, sometimes ambiguous environment and managing multiple priorities What's in it for you We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. Our global benefits package generally includes equity, a wellbeing benefit, a WFH equipment allowance, and an annual Learning & Development stipend. While our processes are fully consolidated into the product development life cycle, we still enjoy a high degree of ownership and autonomy in our work. We’re striving to find the right balance between visual craftsmanship, user needs, and business goals. You’ll apply your experience and education with a customer-centric mindset to solve customer problems and identify business opportunities. We operate in a fast-paced environment and get to have a direct impact on the millions of users who rely on Miro to collaborate and create the next big thing. In our 12-month programme, you'll gain hands-on experience working on real-world projects. You'll also be mentored by experienced designers, researchers, and content writers, who will help you develop your skills and career. As a graduate product designer at Miro, you will focus on learning, growing and bringing a new fresh perspective to the Miro experience. Your goal will be to explore the customer experience, identify opportunities, explore the fields of product design, UXR, and content design, and deliver high-quality solutions but most of all to learn and grow as a new designer at Miro and find your place from Product Design, Research, Content Design or maybe somewhere else. Understand the success of your solutions with the team and iterate on them closely with engineering partners to ensure solid implementation in production. Understand the competitive landscape, and using qualitative and quantitative methods hypothesize new solutions and improvements to the Miro products. Bring delight to Miro users through customer centric solutions implemented thought high-quality interaction design and creative visuals.. What you’ll need Graduate in 2025-6 in Bachelor’s or Master’s in interaction design, industrial design, creative design, HCI, or a related design field Strong drive to learn, and absorb knowledge from your team, and ability to take and utilize this knowledge in your work AI-First Proficiency - We’re building for an AI-first future, and we’re looking for graduates who aren’t just using AI—they’re mastering it. Effective written and verbal communication skills Be available to start a full time position for our 12 month programme from August 2026 Be able to commute to our office in Amsterdam or Berlin What's in it for you We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. title: Design & Motion
description: Design & Motion Film & VFX Watch showreel Watch showreel We offer a full cycle of development and creation of visuals for your product. This could be a company, a service, or even a whole TV channel. What are we doing? We will come up with a concept based on your input, write a script, can also organize filming (if required) and act as an agency \ production \ post-production 3 in 1. We will come up with a concept and storyline for your product (video, event, clip, advertisement) We will prepare various budget options with and without shootings, full CG, or essentially without graphics We will meet all deadlines and ensure transparent work with regular updates and meetings In the initial stages of launching your project, we draw storyboards, create artworks and concepts, develop pre-visualizations, and gradually help you understand how the product will look even if there is no precise vision at the start. Thanks to our international experience, we create content at the forefront of trends and according to the highest industry standards. We are always ready to meet with you or invite you to our cozy office. title: Social Content Creator - Donut Studios (Temporary)
description:
WHY DONUT STUDIOS? 

At New Engen, we help brands grow, not just bigger, but smarter. We’re a digital marketing agency that drives meaningful impact across the entire customer journey by integrating media, creative, analytics, influencer, and retail strategies into performance-driven campaigns.

Donut Studios is our in-house creative studio, purpose-built to craft high-performing, platform-native, and digital content for the world’s most ambitious brands. As a key part of New Engen’s suite of specialized solutions, alongside offerings like the Acorn Creator Suite -  Donut Studios helps unlock breakthrough creative that drives results, fast.

We’re a collaborative group of strategists, content creators, copywriters, editors, designers, account managers, and more who are all in on helping brands, and each other,  thrive. We work hard, stay humble, and build with purpose.

WHAT YOU’LL DO

Are you a hands-on creator who lives and breathes social? We’re looking for a Social Content Creator who’s equal parts strategist, editor, and producer—ready to lead the charge on organic storytelling for our clients, our brand, and our internal creative initiatives.

In this role, you’ll concept, film, and edit content that’s platform-native, trend-aware, and built to perform. From TikTok-first campaigns to behind-the-scenes event content, you’ll bring big ideas to life—quickly and collaboratively.

Here’s what you’ll be diving into day-to-day:

  • Own end-to-end short-form content creation—from hook ideation to final edit
  • Draft scripts, outlines, and shot lists based on creative briefs and goals
  • Self-shoot using mobile or light camera setups, with an eye for lighting and sound
  • Edit for multiple platforms with native pacing, captions, and overlays
  • Monitor daily trends, formats, and audio to inspire new concepts
  • Proactively pitch quick-turn content ideas and seasonal moments
  • Deliver polished assets with correct captions, cover frames, metadata, and filenames
  • Collaborate closely with strategy and copy teams to align on brand voice and performance goals
  • Incorporate feedback quickly and iterate toward a high creative bar
  • Keep projects organized with versioning, assets, and file management
     

WHAT YOU’LL NEED

  • 2–4 years of experience in social content creation, UGC, or short-form production
  • Deep knowledge of TikTok, IG Reels, and Shorts formats—including hook strategies and storytelling structure
  • Comfortable filming yourself and/or directing internal talent on camera
  • Strong editing skills in Adobe Premiere, CapCut, or comparable tools
  • Visual instincts for text overlays, captions, and movement that feel native to the platform
  • A passion for social trends—and the agility to ship same-day ideas
  • Highly organized and collaborative, with the ability to thrive under tight turnarounds
  • An idea-first mindset with a constant flow of bold, unhinged, and culturally relevant concepts
  • A trend-setter mentality with a drive to create and test original formats—not just follow existing trends
  • A distinct sense of humor and personality for crafting engaging content
  • Los Angeles or surrounding area preferred
     

Bonus points fo

title: PRODUCT DESIGNER
description: Pininfarina, prestigioso gruppo multinazionale, market leader in servizi di Stile nel settore Automotive, Product & Experience e Architecture design, per la sua sede di Cambiano (TO), ricerca un/una: PRODUCT DESIGNER La risorsa avrà il compito di assicurare il miglioramento continuo della qualità delle attività tecniche (studi di fattibilità e progettazione) e degli output relativi svolgendo sia un’azione di coaching sulle risorse del team sia partecipando alle riunioni di avanzamento dei progetti promuovendo e verificando lo svolgimento degli studi di fattibilità RESPONSABILITÀ / MANSIONI * Assicurare il supporto tecnico alla progettazione: Collaborare con il team nella realizzazione di progetti, garantendo la precisione nella progettazione sia in 2D che in 3D e contribuendo alla definizione di soluzioni formali e funzionali. Individuare soluzioni di produzione efficaci. * Supporto nella gestione del cliente: Partecipare agli incontri con i clienti, ascoltando attivamente e ponendo domande pertinenti per guidarli verso i piani di lavoro più efficaci ed economici. * Gestione autonoma di aree di responsabilità: Coordinare in autonomia piccole design challenge (1-5 gg), all'interno di progetti, o progetti di piccola scala, garantendo l'esecuzione nei tempi e secondo gli standard aziendali e coordinando i fornitori. * Elaborazione grafica: Produrre sketch e render di immagini work-in-progress per discussioni interne, supportando il team nella comunicazione visiva delle idee progettuali. REQUISITI E SKILLS * Titolo di studio: Laurea in Industrial design o campi correlati. * Esperienza: Esperienza in progetti che coprono l'intero processo progettuale, dalla definizione delle esigenze degli stakeholder alla fase di prototipazione. * Progettazione tecnica: Competenza nella progettazione 2D e 3D, con una profonda comprensione degli strumenti e dei software necessari per il completamento delle attività. * Elaborazione di soluzioni progettuali: Capacità di concettualizzare direzioni progettuali formali e funzionali senza necessità di supervisione. * Scrittura e comunicazione: capacità di scrittura chiara, espressiva e concisa, senza necessità di revisione. * Lingue: Inglese fluente, con capacità di esprimersi in contesti professionali e presentare di fronte a piccoli gruppi. * Giudizio e autonomia: Capacità di identificare ostacoli e opportunità, proponendo soluzioni in autonomia * Coordinamento: Collaborazione con il team per migliorare il lavoro complessivo del progetto e supporto ai colleghi. * Leadership: Capacità di difendere le scelte progettuali, e supporto a designer junior. * Ownership: Definizione autonoma della direzione del lavoro, richiesta spontanea di check-in in caso di necessità * Comunicazione: Contributo chiave alla pianificazione dei progetti e rappresentazione verso l'esterno con supporto minimo. * Feedback: Ricezione di feedback orientata al miglioramento, fornitura di feedback costruttivi ai designer junior per migliorare la loro conoscenza e competenza. * Contributo: Espressione di un punto di vista progettuale forte, con flessibilità nell'incorporare le idee altrui e migliorare il lavoro complessivo. Il presente annuncio è rivolto ad entrambi i sessi, ai sensi delle leggi 903/77 e 125/91, e a persone di tutte le età e tutte le nazionalità, ai sensi dei decreti legislativi 215/03 e 216/03. Saranno presi in considerazione solo i profili che rispettino i requisiti richiesti. Si prega di inviare dettagliato CV indicando l'autorizzazione alla gestione dei dati secondo Regolamento Europeo UE n. 679/2016.

title: Brand & Community Lead
description: Home Careers Share on Brand & Community Lead Pressable is a leading managed WordPress hosting platform trusted by agencies, developers, and businesses with demanding performance and support needs. As part of Automattic, we bring deep WordPress roots and technical excellence to every customer experience. With strong momentum in the mid-market and agency segments, we’re looking for a Brand & Community Lead to define how Pressable shows up in the world → from our visual identity and storytelling to our community presence and activations. About the Role This fully remote role is about building brand love and recognition. You’ll own the Pressable brand voice, identity, and narrative across every channel, making sure everything we publish, sponsor, and share feels unmistakably “Pressable.” You’ll drive awareness, engagement, and advocacy through creative content, community partnerships, and brand activations. That includes our social channels, sponsorships, affiliate and influencer programs, and WordPress community presence—as well as hero content and storytelling that elevate how people perceive our brand. You’ll collaborate closely with Product Marketing, Demand Gen, and Growth to ensure our brand shows up with consistency and intent. You’ll bring new ideas for how to expand our presence through events, content formats, channels, and partnerships. This is a high-visibility, cross-functional role for someone who’s equal parts creative and strategic, with the confidence to steward a brand and the curiosity to experiment with new ways of building awareness. Key Responsibilities Brand Strategy & Identity Own and evolve Pressable’s brand voice, tone, and visual expression across all touchpoints. Ensure consistency and quality across messaging, creative, and customer communications. Lead brand storytelling that differentiates Pressable and deepens trust with our audiences. Content & Storytelling Oversee creation of hero brand content and customer stories. Partner with agencies and freelancers to produce content that elevates our brand and community. Use AI tools to accelerate production, repurpose assets, and maintain editorial consistency. Support launches and campaigns with compelling creative and storytelling assets. Community & Advocacy Own Pressable’s social media presence and grow engagement across all channels. Lead our involvement in the WordPress community, including events, sponsorships, and collaborations. Support and grow our affiliate and influencer programs with new toolkits and campaigns. Develop advocate and ambassador initiatives to turn customers and partners into storytellers. Brand Activations & Events Oversee our brand presence at conferences, WordCamps, and sponsorships. Create event content, experiences, and campaigns that extend reach before and after each event. Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure consistent messaging and creative execution. Measurement & Optimization Define and track brand KPIs (awareness, engagement, reach, share of voice, sentiment). Evaluate ROI of sponsorships, activations, and campaigns—and refine for impact. Partner with Product Marketing, Demand Gen, and Growth to align brand efforts to business goals. You Might Be a Fit If You Have 5–7+ years in brand, content, or community marketing—ideally in B2B SaaS and/or the WordPress community. Have owned a brand’s voice and enjoyed translating it across diverse formats and channels. Are as comfortable leading creative direction as you are managing execution. Have experience building awareness through social, partnerships, and community involvement. Understand how to measure brand health and connect awareness to engagement and growth. Thrive in fast-moving environments and know how to create leverage through AI and automation. Know how to deploy and orchestrate AI agents to extend your reach, automate routine work, and scale creative output. Bonus Points Experience working with agencies, developers, or WordPress professionals. Familiarity with the WordPress community and events like WordCamps. Background in creative direction or storytelling. Experience scaling affiliate or influencer programs. Comfortable using AI tools for creative ideation, editing, or workflow automation. What You’ll Work On Initially Refresh Pressable’s brand guidelines (voice, tone, and visuals). Relaunch our social media strategy and content cadence. Audit and strengthen our WordPress community presence and sponsorships. Explore new affiliate and ambassador toolkits to amplify reach. Oversee development of new hero content, including brand videos, customer stories, and partner narratives. Why Join Pressable?You’ll have the opportunity to shape the future of a growing product with real impact. You’ll work with a small, fast-moving team that values autonomy, clarity, and action → and you’ll do it all within the broader Automattic family. Salary range: $110,000 to $140,000 USD. Please note that while salary ranges are presented here in USD, we will pay in local currency. Location: Remote (North America / UK) #LI-Remote We are searching for high-caliber candidates with the skills and qualities to have a net positive for Automattic. Pay will reflect the potential contribution and the impact you can bring, which may, in some cases, go beyond the range stated for the right candidate. Perks & Benefits of Joining Pressable! Health Benefits for US-based staff (99% Paid Employee Medical, Dental, and Vision). Matching 401(k) for US-based staff. Life and Disability Insurance for US-based staff (100% Paid Life, & LTD). Work from home with home office setup and coworking allowances. Open vacation policy (no set number of days per year). Hardware and software, books or conferences that promote continued learning. So, are you ready to embark on this thrilling WordPress adventure? We can’t wait to welcome you to the Pressable team and empower you to make the web a better place. Come join us in crafting an exceptional customer experience and revolutionizing the world of WordPress hosting. Apply now and let’s build something amazing together! #LI-Remote About Automattic Now in our 20th year, we’re the people behind WordPress.com, WooCommerce, Tumblr, Simplenote, Jetpack, Longreads, Day One, PocketCasts, Beeper, and more. We believe in making the web a better place. We’re a distributed company with more than 1400 Automatticians in nearly every corner of the globe, speaking over a hundred different languages. Enriched by this diversity, we’re united by a singular mission: to democratize publishing, commerce, and messaging so anyone with a story can tell it, anyone with a product can sell it, and everyone can manage their communications from a single source. In short, we help maintain a balance in society, creating and continually refining powerful tools people can use to compete fairly—regardless of income, gender, politics, language, or where they live in the world. We believe in Open Source, and the vast majority of our work is available under the GPL. Automattic is a Most Loved Company and Disability Confident Committed. (Here’s what that might mean for you.) Learn more about our dedication to diversity, equity, and inclusion and our Employee Resource Groups. If you need disability-related accommodations during the application or interview process, please fill out this form. We are committed to ensuring an accessible hiring process for all candidates. To learn about how we handle your data, please review our Privacy Policy. You can track your application status and more at MyGreenhouse. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran. Famous for its unique flora and fauna, award-winning food, whisky and wine, and having the cleanest air in the world, people from all over the world have made Tasmania their home. Enjoy the space to think and reconnect with the luxury of inspiring natural and cultural experiences right on your doorstep. If you’re looking for an ideal place to raise a family in a vibrant yet safe, caring local community — you’ll love Tasmania.Frequently Asked QuestionsWhy aren’t you in Silicon Valley?We could never have made Procreate in the Valley. Tasmania lets us focus on what really matters and affords us the luxury of distance and perspective, so we don't get tied up in trends.Where is Tasmania?Tasmania is an island state off the southeastern coast of the Australian mainland. Recognised as one of the world’s last remaining untouched natural wonders, Tasmania has a diverse and rugged wilderness, with a unique array of wildlife. Tasmania has a cooler climate than the rest of Australia, with four distinct seasons and a wonderfully warm temperate summer.What’s the application process?When it comes to our recruitment process, we're keeping it simple. Our interviews are conversational by design. We're interested in what gives you energy and makes you tick. The goal is to unpack the experiences, skills and motivations that make you, you!It’s a three-stage process:In your first round, you’ll meet with a member of the talent acquisition team.If you’re invited to the 2nd round, you’ll meet with the relevant team lead and dive into the specifics of the role.In the third and final round, you’ll meet with a member of the executive team and a senior from your prospective team.By the end of these three stages, you’ll have developed an in-depth understanding of who we are, what we do and how you can contribute to our journey.Do you support relocation?Yes! We understand relocation is a major commitment and a daunting logistical challenge. We have an experienced team of immigration agents that guide you through the process, start to finish.We also offer significant and ongoing financial support. We’ll pay for your flights. We’ll also pay you a lump-sum allowance that you can use for moving expenses. Once you're here, we’ll provide you with short-term accommodation while you look for a place to call home.On top of that, we’ll provide private health insurance for you and your direct family. You’ll remain covered until you become eligible for national health insurance. Relocation can be challenging but it's also an adventure. We'll be by your side for the whole journey.In the newsAcclaimed digital brush creator Kyle T. title: Apple Design Awards 2024: another win for Procreate
description:
Art Digital Art Digital Art Software Apple Design Awards 2024: another win for Procreate News By Joe Foley published 8 June 2024 And Vision Pro apps get a look in with a new spatial computing category. Subscribe to our newsletter The winners are in for the Apple Design Awards 2024, and we're not surprised to see a third accolade for Procreate. This time the team behind the digital art app won the app innovation award for Procreate Dreams, a powerful animation app for iPad (see our own five-star Procreate Dreams review. Elsewhere in this year's awards, there was a new category, spatial computing, following the launch of Apple's Vision Pro headset. In total, 14 apps and games won awards for design, technical achievement and innovation. Procreate Dreams is a 2D animation app for iPad (Image credit: Procreate)The winners of the Apple Design Awards 2024 were chosen from 42 finalists. One app and one game were chosen in each of seven categories: delight and fun, inclusivity, innovation, interaction, social impact, visuals and graphics and the new spatial computing category. You may like The best drawing apps for iPad Procreate Dreams 2 makes the iPad animation app even more powerful and versatile From Wicked to KPop Demon Hunters, see the Concept Art Awards 2025 winners in full Having already its second accolade in the Apple Design Awards 2022, Procreate picked up the innovation award for apps with Procreate Dreams. Apple praised the 2D animation app for including the extensive library of brushes, gestures, and PencilKit-enabled behaviors from the original Procreate and for its support for multitouch interactions and Apple Pencil.Winners in the visuals and graphics category were Rooms from Things Inc, an app and social space for building imaginative scenes, and Lies of P from NEOWIZ, an adventure game that allows players to control a robotic puppet. Here Apple commended the textures, detailed lighting and effects and visual customization options like MetalFX upscaling and volumetric fog effects on Mac. Algoriddim's Djay Pro won the inaugural edition of the spatial computing category (Image credit: Algoriddim)The winners in the new spatial computing category were Algoriddim's Djay Pro - DJ App & AI Mixer, a virtual DJ app complete with turntables, interactive effects and a range of virtual environments, and Shapes and Stories' Blackbox a puzzle game that encourages users to explore the spatial environment. Apply now Copy Apply now CopyWe’re looking for our a dedicated Engineering Manager to help lead teams in pursuit of our ambitious product roadmap. We're driven by the desire to deliver the best creative software experience for our global community.You’ll have deep technical experience as well as ability in progressive people management. You'll guide a small team of engineers to develop and ship mission critical features, solve complex technical problems, improve development efficiency and nurture our internal engineering talent.The roleThe role sits within our product development team, and you’ll report to Michael Shaw, our CTO. You'll work closely with the engineering team, the executive and members of other departments to align engineering with other critical business functions. What you'll doLead, coordinate and align engineering functions.Work closely with design to help plan and organise development of features to improve sustainability and efficiency in our development process.Deliver feedback and take part in reviews and performance management.Hands-on engineering will be 40-60% of the role - we need an Engineering Manager that enjoys coding and wants to keep their engineering skills sharp.Required SkillsEmotional intelligence and a calm, self-assured leadership style. You’ll have a demonstrated ability to manage and build diverse teams. Your approach to leadership and management will build consensus and understanding. You’ll be able to communicate technical information to a diverse set of non-technical employees. You’ll motivate, inspire and support your colleagues to produce the best work of their lives.Experience as an Engineering Manager in a technology, product or creative company. You’ll have had experience in engineering at a senior level and/or people management experience in a tech or start-up environment. You bring an organised, methodical and detail-oriented approach to your work which provides structure and promotes confidence in those you work with. Your work will draw the best out of each individual contributor in a way which promotes both individual success and engagement as well as team productivity.Experience with Swift and iOS.Good technical judgement and a track record of making technically grounded decisions.Excellent verbal and written communication skills.Bonus SkillsExperience with diverse languages that have alternate paradigms to Swift (Haskell/C/Rust).What to expect in the first 6-12 monthsComplete the Procreate onboarding process and get to know your colleagues.Build an understanding of our product development practices, processes and frameworks.Begin to develop relationships with key stakeholders, team leaders and members of the executive team.Prepare and support the execution of core featuresPlay your part in shipping products.We want to hear from youWe’d love to hear about how you see yourself contributing to Procreate in this role. Please note, we're looking for full-time and on-site employees for our Hobart or Sydney office. Apply now to be considered for the role!Work at ProcreateWe make tools that millions of people use to express their innate human creativity. We’re working to make them accessible to millions more.See all positionsSee our FAQ

title: A Revitalized Brand for a ConnectedSkateboarding Community
description: Building upon this foundation, a rebranding was carried out that incorporated the symbolism, keeping the globe from the original brand as a starting point but introducing the idea of two worlds merging. Simple, versatile logo reflects unity and diversity at The Berrics’ core. Created for diverse product range, drawing from original brand. Examples showcase its adaptability and versatility. A detailed brand manual was developed, establishing rules and best practices for the proper use of the new identity. Building on this foundation, a rebranding effort introduced a logo that retained the original globe but reimagined it to symbolize the merging of two worlds. The result is a simple, versatile logo that reflects the unity and diversity at Berrics’ core. Designed to cover a broad product range, it draws on the minimalism of the original brand while adding expressive touches. description: Creative Team (Art Director & Copywriter) Sao PauloSHAREXLINKEDINEMAILCreative Team - Art Director & Copywriter About R/GA R/GA is a global digital innovation agency specializing in digital product development, brand transformation, media, and communications for major companies. We are on a mission to help companies grow by enabling them to change—across their business, the experience they offer, and the campaigns that take them to market. Our work blends what we call Stories and Systems: narrative and design, technology and interface working together. R/GA is a brand, but also a collective of atypical outsiders and misfits who drive everything we do through bold creativity and bright, strategic thinking. About the Role R/GA is searching for a creative team - Art Director & Copywriter - who together deliver world-class ideas that push the boundaries of creativity. You’re fluent in concepting as a team and collaborating with others. You know how to turn insights into powerful ideas and bring them to life through impeccable craft, whether in words, visuals, or integrated experiences. You’re big on big ideas, comfortable selling them to clients. You're endlessly curious, inventive, and at ease working across formats, channels, and technologies. Our creative teams think broadly—about culture, behavior, platforms, and emerging tech. They craft brand stories and systems, and connect people with brands through work that’s original, unexpected, strategic, and always on brand. On any given day you might - Concept and collaborate as a team (AD + CW) and with other creatives including designers, strategists, creative directors, technologists, and project managers.. - Develop campaign platforms and translate them into integrated executions across channels—film, social, digital ecosystems, product experiences, activations, content, and more. - Craft world-class copy (CW) and world-class visual expressions (AD): from headlines and scripts to design systems, art direction, and layouts. - Explore and apply new technologies, from AI tools to emerging platforms, in the service of idea development and execution. - Work closely with creative leadership from the NY office to ensure concepts are on brand and strategically grounded. - Partner with project managers to ensure deliverables are on time, on budget, and aligned with client expectations. - Present ideas to clients at all levels with clarity, conviction, and a strong creative point of view. - Guide and mentor junior talent, fostering a collaborative and high-performing creative culture. The Ideal Team - A portfolio demonstrating conceptual strength and the ability to produce innovative, award-winning work across channels. - Work that shows outstanding craft—writing craft (CW) and design/visual craft (AD). - Curiosity, passion for digital media, and an interest in emerging tech such as AI, platform innovation, and new creative tools. - The ability to juggle multiple projects and maintain quality under pressure. - Strong collaboration and communication skills—able to accept feedback, iterate, and elevate ideas together. - A desire to build brands, solve business problems creatively, and produce work that earns attention and results. - Marketing sense and an understanding of how campaign platforms work across systems and touchpoints. You Bring - A portfolio showcasing stories + systems thinking and strong idea-to-execution capabilities. - 3 to 6 years of experience working at world-class agencies and/or on global brands. - Ability to collaborate seamlessly across departments (strategy, tech, PM, production, media). - Fluency with writing tools (CW) and design/execution platforms (AD). - Strong ideation and presentation skills that convince, sell, and inspire. - Proficiency in English (written & verbal). - Academic background in Advertising, Communications, Design, Copywriting, or equivalent experience.

description: Senior Experience Designer / Hybrid / onsite in LondonLondonSHAREXLINKEDINEMAIL R/GA EMEA Senior Experience Designer Location: London / Hybrid About R/GA R/GA is an independent creative innovation company built for the intelligence age. We harness the power of design and technology to create more valuable experiences for people and brands. From reimagining interfaces with AI to launching entirely new platforms and services, we help organisations adapt to what’s next and shape what comes after. Our teams combine craft, curiosity and technology to deliver work that drives both business and human impact. At R/GA, we don’t just respond to change — we help shape it. The Role This is a craft-driven role for someone who can confidently tackle today’s experience design challenges while helping shape the interfaces of the future. It’s about designing experiences that are intuitive, intelligent and inclusive, grounded in solid UX principles and open to evolution. Experimentation is essential; we’re looking for someone who questions convention, embraces emerging technologies and contributes to defining new standards in an AI-driven world. We’re also looking for people who bring energy and ambition. Designers who care deeply about the craft, get excited by complex problems, and see design as a superpower. You’ll work closely with strategy, technology, creative and client teams to turn insight into innovation. Whether you’re mapping a product journey, prototyping a new interaction, or pushing the limits of what AI tools can do, you’ll bring a clear perspective on how technology can unlock better experiences for people and brands. On any given day you might be Lead the UX design on products, services and platforms across a range of industries Explore how AI-powered interactions (e.g. conversational UIs, generative interfaces, smart automation) can deliver more human experiences Design experiences that evolve and adapt to human interactions Use generative technology and other tools to concept, prototype, and test ideas faster and smarter Translate research into clear user flows, wireframes, and design systems Collaborate with visual designers, strategists, technologists and clients to shape the work Facilitate workshops and collaborative working sessions Present and rationalise design decisions to internal teams and external stakeholders Contribute to how we build, lead and evolve design teams at R/GA You’d be the right fit if you Are curious about what’s next for UX and see AI as a key part of that evolution Have experience launching digital products or services that put people first Bring a clear, structured approach to problem-solving Have the ability to turn creative concepts into clear, actionable design Think in systems and patterns, not just screens Are comfortable working in an agile, collaborative, fast-moving environment Can clearly articulate your ideas, rationale and ambition Are excited to experiment with AI-powered design tools, and build your own Have a strong sense of craft, care about the details, and take pride in making things better You bring 4+ years of experience in UX, product, service or interaction design A portfolio that shows thoughtful, real-world design solutions and the thinking behind them Mastery of Figma, Miro, Google Workspace and other prototyping and collaborative tools Strong prototyping skills with the ability to quickly bring ideas to life and explore interactions at different levels of fidelity Hands-on experience with AI tools such as ChatGPT, Midjourney, Runway, ElevenLabs, GitHub Copilot, or your own custom stack A track record of using AI in your design process, whether through prototyping, testing, or building internal tools A solid understanding of interaction design principles, accessibility, and inclusive design A drive to contribute to the culture of a progressive, multidisciplinary team Diversity Everyone is welcome at R/GA, and we are committed to empowering our talent to bring their authentic self to work. title: Senior Designer
description: Creative WHO WE ARE At Sullivan, we’re driven by fearless curiosity. Around here, asking bold questions, understanding what makes people tick, imagining fresh approaches to solve complex problems, and thinking creatively are all in a day’s work. Together, we help organizations of consequence build and activate brands that move audiences to action. WHAT YOUR DAY-TO-DAY WILL LOOK LIKE As a Senior Designer, you are someone who is both a strong conceptual thinker and a systems-minded practitioner—able to pair big ideas with rigorous, interconnected thinking. Your day-to-day will include: Providing creative vision from concept to execution, for one of our largest accounts, American Express, with comfort spanning from building a brand identity to rolling out cohesive marketing campaigns across all channels. Collaborating with writers, strategists, and producers on various client projects. Bringing fresh ideas and experimenting in unexpected ways to push the boundaries of design. Solving design problems with a hands-on approach while also overseeing designers and delegating to them to bring big creative ideas to life. Using typography, color, photography, and illustration to build on existing guidelines or design from scratch. Creating assets, components, and systems for various platforms that are beautiful, easy to use, and strategically sound. Presenting your designs and communicating your decisions to both internal teams and clients. WHAT YOU’LL BRING TO THE TEAM 5-8 years agency experience creating and presenting strategic design solutions. Relevant design degree and a strong portfolio. Excellent conceptual, composition, art direction, layout, and typography skills. Skilled at flexing design styles to follow briefs and brand identity standards. Confidence in your work and the ability to defend it both strategically and conceptually. Passion for uncovering big strategic ideas to produce out-of-the-box creative. Interest in sharing new insights, methods, and innovations that enrich our creative culture. Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite and Figma. A basic knowledge digital & social media best practices and curiosity about these evolving platforms. HOW WE’LL SUPPORT YOU Close-knit team of ideators who love what we do and bring our full selves to work. Both autonomy to work your way and access to leadership and mentors. Opportunities to share ideas, tackle challenges, and jump in to make consequential creative work. Fun environment with themed happy hours, game nights, and a collaborative spirit that runs through our office and online. Hybrid work model that values work-life balance, competitive benefits, and an entrepreneurial culture where you can make your own path. Please Submit Resume to careers@sullivannyc.com Share .
