title: Content Marketing Manager
description:
Overview


TrustArc is looking for a strategic, hands-on Content Marketing Manager to drive content that builds awareness, supports demand generation, and positions TrustArc as a leader in privacy and governance. This role creates compelling content for privacy, legal, security, and executive audiences across the full buyer journey. The ideal candidate is a strong storyteller who understands how content drives pipeline and revenue, and is able to report on it. This position reports to the VP of Demand Generation.

\n


What You'll Do
  • Content Strategy: Lead the development and execution of a content strategy aligned to revenue goals and priority ICPs. Own and manage the cross-channel editorial calendar to ensure consistency and impact, while aligning major content initiatives to product launches, regulatory developments, and key market moments.
  • Demand Generation Support: Partner with internal stakeholders and external resources to create high-performing demand assets, including blogs, eBooks, guides, and landing pages. Ensure all content is optimized for both paid distribution and organic discovery to maximize reach, engagement, and lead conversion.
  • Thought Leadership: Elevate TrustArc executives as credible industry voices by developing strong POV content focused on privacy, AI governance, and regulatory change. Build a consistent thought leadership presence that supports brand authority and drives strategic conversations in the market.
  • SEO & Performance: Own the SEO-driven content roadmap and continuously optimize website content for both search visibility and conversion performance. Track engagement and pipeline impact, using performance insights to refine strategy and improve ROI.
  • Collaboration: Work closely with Product Marketing to ensure alignment on messaging and positioning, and manage external writers or agencies as needed to scale content production effectively.


What You'll Bring
  • 5+ years of B2B SaaS content marketing experience.
  • Strong writing and editing skills.
  • Proven ability to tie content to pipeline results.
  • Extensive experience with Google Analytics and analytics tools. Ability to interpret website and blog statistics and identify areas for optimization.
  • Familiarity with Marketo and Salesforce.
  • Ability to translate complex topics into clear, compelling content.


What We Offer
  • Health, Vision, and Dental Care (also available for partner).
  • Endless PTO Program.
  • 100% Work from Home.
  • Opportunities to participate in health-focused activities - mindfulness, wellness, active lifestyle.
  • $2,500 active employee referral program

Compensation: up to $90,000 per year
Actual compensation within the range will be dependent upon the individual's skills, experience, qualifications and location, and applicable employment laws.


\n

​​About Us


As the leader in data privacy, TrustArc automates and simplifies the creation of end-to-end privacy management programs for global organizations. TrustArc is the only company to deliver the depth of privacy intelligence, coupled with the complete platform automation, that is essential for the growing number of privacy regulations in an ever-changing digital world. We have a global team across the Americas, Europe, and Asia, TrustArc helps customers worldwide demonstrate compliance, minimize risk, and build trust. For additional information visit www.trustarc.com.


We invest in our teammember’s work-life balance, with unlimited paid time off, flexible working hours, and a work from home environment. We support employees with their own industry specific learning. We have weekly virtual team events such as trivia games, celebrating “international pet day”, lunch’n’learns, exercise classes, Women at TrustArc ERG, and many more.


All of our above initiatives and employee support is in line with our seven core values: Customer Obsessed, Fearless, Accountable, Collaborative, Agile, Transparent and Effective. We like to encourage our employees to end each day with the question: “Which value(s) did I demonstrate today?” And we acknowledge exceptional work through our semi-annual awards, which includes a paid vacation!


Equal Opportunity


TrustArc is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace. We believe that a successful organization is one that celebrates its employees for who they are, who they love, and the unique lens through which they experience the world. TrustArc does not discriminate based on race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, Veteran status, or other legally protected status. We understand that our differences make us better. They empower us to learn from each other, lean on each other, and create a product and community that models the behavior we hope to see in others.


If you need reasonable accommodation to search for a job opening, apply for a position, or participate in the interview process, contact us at hr@trustarc.com and describe the specific accommodation requested for a disability-related limitation. Reasonable accommodations are modifications or adjustments to the application or hiring process that would enable you to fully participate in that process.

title: Design Engineer
description: Back to jobsDesign EngineerRemote - United States About Vercel: Vercel gives developers the tools and cloud infrastructure to build, scale, and secure a faster, more personalized web. As the team behind v0, Next.js, and AI SDK, Vercel helps customers like Ramp, Supreme, PayPal, and Under Armour build for the AI-native web. Our mission is to enable the world to ship the best products. That starts with creating a place where everyone can do their best work. Whether you're building on our platform, supporting our customers, or shaping our story: You can just ship things.About the Role: The design team at Vercel is comprised of capable and considerate humans working to solve challenges that are unique, complex, and nuanced—just like the web. Vercel is looking for an experienced individual to join the Design Engineering team at Senior or Staff level. The Design Engineering team is responsible for the highest level of polish, creativity and interaction on Vercel. You will work across the logged out experience of Vercel,shaping the future of Vercel.com, events, and marketing surfaces that introduce the world to Vercel. You'll work on design initiatives and create thoughtful, innovative, systems-based solutions for top-of-funnel experiences, working closely with design, marketing, sales, GTM, Analytics, product design, and engineering. Designers at Vercel have broad creative license, and the freedom to create and lead new, design-driven projects. If you are someone who gets excited about the possibility of creative development, uncovering insights to drive conversion, improving user experience, experimenting, and personalizing, then this might be the right role for you. If you’re based within a pre-determined commuting distance of one of our offices (SF, NY, London, or Berlin), the role includes in-office anchor days on Monday, Tuesday, and Friday. If you're located beyond that distance, the role is fully remote. For location-specific details, please connect with our recruiting team. What You Will Do: Report to our Director of Design Engineering and help Vercel strengthen its acquisition surfaces by driving design leadership and engineering excellence on vercel.com, events, and related experiences. Contribute to the design process by gathering requirements, planning and ideating from the start of a project, and shaping modular systems for repeatable marketing and growth use cases. Demonstrate an understanding of the development workflow, CMS and analytics integrations (Payload, Rudderstack, Clearbit, Sequel.io, etc.), and how to streamline processes with cross-functional partners. Work with designers, marketers, and engineers to collaborate on ideation and ensure high-quality, performant implementations across campaigns and pages. Help lead the technical roadmap for acquisition surfaces, championing iterative improvements to browser performance, accessibility, Core Web Vitals, and SEO. Provide design systems feedback and iteration to help empower the entire company with a first-class, consistent web experience. Advocate for and mentor other design engineers, sharing best practices in frontend craft, animation, experimentation, and performance. Help grow the team and elevate design and engineering within the company. About You: Depth and quality of your experience is more important than years. That said, successful candidates typically have at least 5+ years of development experience. Strong programming skills, experience with application development, and excellent troubleshooting skills. Excellent written and verbal communication skills with a keen eye for detail. Excellent understanding of Next.js and Vercel, CMS platforms like Payload (or Contentful), analytics platforms like Amplitude or Heap, and design tools like Figma. Familiarity with growth experimentation, SEO principles, and marketing/brand campaigns. Skills and understanding on graphic design (layout, typography, color, illustration). Perfectionism with the experience (polish, readability, accessibility, performance). Experience working in or with Software as a Service (SaaS) companies. You have closely collaborated with folks from marketing and design in previous roles. Benefits: Competitive compensation package, including equity. Inclusive Healthcare Package. Learn and Grow - we provide mentorship and send you to events that help you build your network and skills. Flexible Time Off. We will provide you the gear you need to do your role, and a WFH budget for you to outfit your space as needed. The San Francisco, CA base pay range for this role is $196,000-$294,000. Actual salary will be based on job-related skills, experience, and location. Compensation outside of San Francisco may be adjusted based on employee location. The total compensation package may include benefits, equity-based compensation, and eligibility for a company bonus or variable pay program depending on the role. Your recruiter can share more details during the hiring process. Vercel is committed to fostering and empowering an inclusive community within our organization. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, gender expression or identity, sexual orientation, national origin, citizenship, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Vercel encourages everyone to apply for our available positions, even if they don't necessarily check every box on the job description.

description:

Performance Marketing Designer 

Salary Range: $65,000 - $75,000 CAD Annually

Overview

The Performance Marketing Designer is responsible for designing high-impact, results-driven creative for our always-on marketing channels, with a primary focus on weekly BAU ads and lifecycle email flows. This role blends strong design craft, performance awareness, and rapid iteration to deliver creative that supports growth, conversion, and brand consistency.

Reporting into the Head of Creative and working closely with the Performance Marketing team, this is a highly collaborative, execution-focused role. The ideal candidate is a proactive idea generator, a strong visual problem solver, and a designer who is motivated by testing, learning, and continuously improving creative based on real performance insights.

What You'll Do: 

  • Design and deliver high-performing creative for BAU paid ads across key performance channels, with a strong focus on speed, quality, and consistency.
  • Support the creation and optimization of email flows and lifecycle communications, ensuring strong visual clarity, brand alignment, and performance impact.
  • Support the design and optimization of performance landing pages, partnering with Performance and Copy to create clear, conversion-focused layouts, visual systems, and asset iterations that improve on-page performance.
  • Partner closely with the Performance Marketing team to understand objectives, audiences, and channel needs, and translate those into effective creative solutions.
  • Take an iterative, test-and-learn approach to creative, regularly refreshing, optimizing, and evolving assets based on performance data and insights.
  • Actively contribute ideas for new ad concepts, formats, hooks, and visual approaches that can improve performance and scale.
  • Deep dive into what is working and what is not, using performance insights to inform design decisions and creative direction.
  • Adapt creative for different platforms, formats, and placements while maintaining strong brand consistency and clarity.
  • Capture and produce lightweight, lo-fi creative when needed (e.g., iPhone photo/video, quick UGC-style content, rapid turn social assets) to support fast testing, reactive moments, and performance iterations.
  • Support campaign work when needed, contributing performance-minded executions within larger brand initiatives.
  • Manage multiple workstreams simultaneously, ensuring timelines, priorities, and deliverables are met with a high standard of execution.
  • Prepare files accurately for delivery across digital channels, ensuring assets are production-ready and meet technical specifications.
  • Collaborate closely with Creative, Performance, Copy, and Channel partners to ensure alignment and strong execution.
  • Maintain organized files, clear versioning, and efficient workflows to support fast-paced production and iteration.
  • Continuously look for ways to improve creative quality, efficiency, and impact through process, craft, and experimentation.
  • Continuously evolve creative skillsets through inspiration, experimentation, research, and upskilling.
  • Explore and integrate new tools, production techniques, and emerging technologies, including AI, to enhance ideation, workflows, editing craft, and execution, aligned with creative direction.

What We're Looking For: 

  • 3+ years of professional experience in graphic design, marketing design, or performance-focused creative roles.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Graphic Design, Visual Communication, Digital Media, Marketing, or a related field.
  • Demonstrated excellence in design principles, including typography, layout, composition, visual hierarchy, color, and brand systems.
  • High proficiency in Figma and Adobe Creative Suite.
  • Proven experience designing for paid media and digital marketing channels, including advertising and email communications.
  • Strong capabilities in motion, animation, and video editing, with experience producing motion-led and video-first assets for digital platforms.
  • Solid understanding of platform-native creative, including format requirements, pacing, and visual storytelling.
  • Strong awareness of culture, design, and social trends, with the ability to translate insights into relevant and effective creative solutions.
  • Highly organized and capable of managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced, iterative environment.
  • Demonstrated ability to take ownership of work, identify opportunities, and drive initiatives forward with a high degree of accountability.
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to deliver accurate, production-ready files.
  • Effective communicator and collaborative partner across creative, marketing, and performance teams.
  • Experience with content capture, motion graphics or 3D tools is an asset.

title: Senior / Staff Software Engineer, Motion Planning
description:
Waabi, founded by AI visionary Raquel Urtasun, is the leader in Physical AI. With a world-class team, we're unlocking the next era of autonomous transportation with technology that's powering commercial autonomous trucks and robotaxis. Waabi is backed by and partners with world leaders in AI, automotive, logistics, and deep tech.


With offices in Toronto, San Francisco, Dallas, and Pittsburgh, Waabi is growing quickly and looking for diverse, innovative and collaborative candidates who want to impact the world in a positive way. To learn more visit: www.waabi.ai


You will...

- Design and develop motion planning software and algorithms, building the brain which orchestrates decision making in dynamic, uncertain and interactive traffic scenarios.

- Be part of a team of multidisciplinary Engineers and Research Scientists using an AI-first approach to enable safe self-driving at scale.

- Participate and share ideas in technical and architecture discussions, collaborating with researchers and engineers to build a reliable autonomy stack for self-driving vehicles.


Qualifications:

- MS/PhD or Bachelor degree with a minimum of 4 years of industry experience in Computer Science, Robotics and/or similar technical field(s) of study.

- Experience in planning/decision making  approaches (e.g., optimal control, optimization based approaches, search methods, probabilistic decision making).

- Production-quality coding skill in Python or C++.

- Solid problem solving skills using linear algebra, geometry, statistics & probability.

- Ability to rapidly prototype and test new algorithms.

- Open-minded and collaborative team player with the willingness to help others.

- Able to maintain progress towards long-term goals while working on short-term problems.

- Passionate about self-driving technologies, solving hard problems, and creating innovative solutions.

 

Bonus/nice to have:

- Experience working in self-driving field.

- Experience in optimization.

\n


\n

The US yearly salary range for this role is: $158,000 - $269,000 USD in addition to competitive perks & benefits. Waabi US Inc.’s yearly salary ranges are determined based on several factors in accordance with the Company’s compensation practices. The salary base range is reflective of the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position across all US locations.  Note: The Company provides additional compensation for employees in this role, including equity incentive awards and an annual performance bonus.


Perks/Benefits:

- Competitive compensation and equity awards.

- Health and Wellness benefits encompassing Medical, Dental and Vision coverage (for full-time employees only).

- Unlimited Vacation.

- Flexible hours and Work from Home support.

- Daily drinks, snacks and catered meals (when in office).

- Regularly scheduled team building activities and social events both on-site, off-site & virtually.

- As we grow, this list continues to evolve! 


Waabi is a technology start-up building technologies to transform the way the world moves. Join our talented team to be a part of the future and to make an impact!


Waabi is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating a supportive, inclusive, and accessible workplace for all our employees. We seek applicants of all backgrounds and identities, across race, color, ethnicity, national origin or ancestry, age, citizenship, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, military or veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or parental status, caregiver status, disability, or any other characteristic protected by law. We make workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process please let our recruiting team know.

title: Brand Media Lead
description: Back to jobsBrand Media LeadCA Remote (BC & ON only); U.S. Remote At Webflow, we’re building the world’s leading AI-native Digital Experience Platform, and we’re doing it as a remote-first company built on trust, transparency, and a whole lot of creativity. This work takes grit, because we move fast, without ever sacrificing craft or quality. Our mission is to bring development superpowers to everyone. From entrepreneurs launching their first idea to global enterprises scaling their digital presence, we empower teams to design, launch, and optimize for the web without barriers. We believe the future of the web, and work, is more open, more creative, and more equitable. And we’re here to build it together. We're looking for a Brand Media Lead to own the strategy, planning, and execution of Webflow's brand media investments. This role sits at the intersection of brand strategy and media execution, translating campaign objectives into high-impact media plans that build awareness, shift perception, and drive consideration among our target audiences. You'll partner closely with Brand Strategy leadership to understand campaign briefs, then develop and execute media strategies across channels, including connected TV (OTT), audio, programmatic display, and emerging formats. You'll manage agency and vendor relationships, negotiate media buys, and work with Marketing Analytics to measure impact through MMM, brand lift studies, and other measurement frameworks. This is a strategic, hands-on role for someone who loves both the art and science of media—understanding the nuances of different channels while staying rigorous about measurement and optimization. You'll have the opportunity to shape how a fast-growing brand shows up in the market and build the infrastructure for brand media at Webflow. About the role: Location: Remote-first (United States; BC & ON, Canada) Full-time Permanent Exempt The cash compensation for this role is tailored to align with the cost of labor in different geographic markets. We've structured the base pay ranges for this role into zones for our geographic markets, and the specific base pay within the range will be determined by the candidate’s geographic location, job-related experience, knowledge, qualifications, and skills. United States (all figures cited below are in USD and pertain to workers in the United States) Zone A: $185,000 - $223,000 Zone B: $176,000 - $212,000 Zone C: $167,000 - $201,000 Canada (figures cited below are in CAD and pertain to workers in ON & BC, Canada) 185,000 - 223,000 This role is also eligible to participate in Webflow's company-wide bonus program. Target amounts are a percentage of base salary and vary by career level. Payouts are based on company performance against established financial and operational goals. Please visit our Careers page for more information on which locations are included in each of our geographic pay zones. However, please confirm the zone for your specific location with your recruiter. Application Information: Application deadline: applications accepted on an ongoing basis until position is closed and filled This posting is for a new position Reporting to the Director, Growth Marketing As a Brand Media Lead you’ll … Media Strategy & Planning Translate brand briefs into comprehensive media plans, including channel selection, targeting strategy, reach/frequency goals, flighting, and geographic consideration Research and recommend channels and vendors based on audience behavior, campaign objectives, and budget efficiency Develop buying strategies across programmatic, reservation-based, and direct buys; determine optimal CPM, CPV, and other buying methods Stay current on media trends, emerging platforms, and innovations in brand advertising (CTV, retail media, audio, digital OOH, etc.) Execution & Optimization Negotiate and finalize media plans with vendors, securing favorable rates and added-value opportunities Direct contractor resources in execution, including trafficking, budget management, and campaign setup Manage live campaigns to ensure delivery against goals; troubleshoot pacing issues and optimize in-flight performance Ensure brand safety and viewability standards are met across all placements Measurement & Reporting Partner with Marketing Analytics to design measurement frameworks, including brand lift studies, MMM title: Staff Product Designer, New Products
description: Back to jobsStaff Product Designer, New ProductsCA Remote (BC & ON only); U.S. Remote At Webflow, we’re building the world’s leading AI-native Digital Experience Platform, and we’re doing it as a remote-first company built on trust, transparency, and a whole lot of creativity. This work takes grit, because we move fast, without ever sacrificing craft or quality. Our mission is to bring development superpowers to everyone. From entrepreneurs launching their first idea to global enterprises scaling their digital presence, we empower teams to design, launch, and optimize for the web without barriers. We believe the future of the web, and work, is more open, more creative, and more equitable. And we’re here to build it together. We’re looking for a Staff Product Designer to help us lead end-to-end design across the Develop pillar—crafting AI-native workflows and systemized patterns that help builders move faster with confidence. You’ll partner with Engineering, Product, and Insights to clarify ambiguous problems, prototype solutions, and ship high-quality experiences that scale across surfaces. About the role: Location: Remote-first (United States; BC & ON) Full-time Permanent Exempt The cash compensation for this role is tailored to align with the cost of labor in different geographic markets. We've structured the base pay ranges for this role into zones for our geographic markets, and the specific base pay within the range will be determined by the candidate’s geographic location, job-related experience, knowledge, qualifications, and skills. United States (all figures cited below are in USD and pertain to workers in the United States) Zone A: $187,000 - $238,000 Zone B: $175,000 - $223,000 Zone C: $164,000 - $209,000 Canada (figures cited below are in CAD and pertain to workers in ON & BC, Canada 212,000 - 270,000 This role is also eligible to participate in Webflow's company-wide bonus program. Target amounts are a percentage of base salary and vary by career level. Payouts are based on company performance against established financial and operational goals. Please visit our Careers page for more information on which locations are included in each of our geographic pay zones. However, please confirm the zone for your specific location with your recruiter. Application Information: Application deadline: applications accepted on an ongoing basis until position is closed and filled This posting is for an existing vacancy Reporting to the Senior Product Design Manager As a Staff Product Designer, you’ll … Define, design, and launch new products across complex, technical domains while seamlessly integrating with existing capabilities. Proactively collaborate across teams to untangle complex, AI-focused technical and user experience dependencies. Present your work to stakeholders and senior leadership, actioning feedback and driving alignment on design vision and strategy. Regularly engage with customers to develop a thorough understanding of their needs and pain points. Continually elevate the craft of design at Webflow and be a thought leader for high impact design decisions. About you: Requirements: BA/BS degree or equivalent experience You’ll thrive as a Staff Product Designer if you: Are fluent in agentic product experiences, have a good understanding of how they work technically, and are excited to build them for Webflow users. Have demonstrated experience influencing and leading complex initiatives in a remote environment. Communicate clearly, drive alignment across functions, and maintain momentum through ambiguity. Are familiar with the web design industry, content management platforms, web standards, and accessibility. Have a bias towards action working on complex products at a high growth, scaling company. Sweat the details to ensure that the products we ship continuously exceed our customer’s expectations. Care about the impact of high quality software experiences, and are comfortable with influencing business strategy that drives tangible growth. Possess a strong growth mindset, are optimistic about the future and comfortable with ambiguity. Stay curious and open to growth — actively building fluency in emerging technologies like AI to unlock creativity, accelerate progress, and amplify impact. Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for full-time employees and their dependents, with Webflow covering most premiums. Support for every stage of family life. 12 weeks of paid parental leave for all parents and 6+ weeks of additional paid leave for birthing parents. Plus inclusive care for family planning, menopause, and midlife transitions. Time off that’s actually off. Flexible vacation, paid holidays, and a sabbatical program to help you recharge and come back inspired. Wellness for the whole you. Access to mental health resources, therapy and coaching. Invest in your future. A 401(k) with 100% employer match (up to $6,000/year) in the U.S., and support for retirement savings globally. Monthly stipends that flex with your life. Localized support for work and wellness expenses — from Wi-Fi to workouts. Bonus for building together. All full-time, permanent, non-commission employees are eligible for our annual WIN bonus program. Temporary employees may be eligible for paid holiday and time off, statutory leaves of absence, and company-sponsored medical benefits depending on their Fixed Term Contract and their country/state of employment. title: Creative Director - Visual & Motion Design
description: <p>As a Creative Director at AKQA (part of WPP)&nbsp;you will bridge creative vision and execution across static and motion media. You will lead and mentor a team of UI/UX, visual &amp; motion designers, define high-quality visual standards, drive storytelling through creative ideation and ensure cohesive brand expression across campaigns, digital platforms, film/animation, and interactive media. You will partner closely with creative leadership, strategy, clients (internal or external), and production to deliver compelling, on-brand visual experiences.</p>\n<p>Your role is critical in elevating the visual voice of the agency, championing innovation in design, and translating big ideas into polished, emotionally engaging visual and animated work.</p>\n<p>Location - Mumbai&nbsp;</p>\n<p><strong>Role Requirements</strong></p>\n<ul>\n<li>\n<p>12+ years of experience with a strong, diverse portfolio showcasing visual design, motion graphics, storytelling, animation, brand work, interactive pieces, and campaign-level thinking</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Expert proficiency in Adobe Creative Cloud (After Effects, Premiere Pro, Illustrator, Photoshop)</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Skilled in animation, compositing, visual effects, motion typography</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Experience in 3D tools (Cinema 4D, Blender, Maya, or equivalent) preferred</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Understanding of UI / UX motion (app/web animations)</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Ability to work with Lottie / vector motion / web-embedded assets is a plus</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>You should be able to translate strategy or messaging into visually compelling narratives—static or motion</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Ability to lead a team, guide, critique, mentor junior &amp; mid-level designers, and uphold quality and consistency across teams</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Comfortable working with creative directors, strategists, copywriters, producers, developers, and clients</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Ability to articulate design rationale and present work convincingly</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Ensuring final deliverables meet aesthetic, technical, and brand standards</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Stay updated on motion, design trends, tools, and evolving formats, bring fresh ideas and push visual boundaries</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Be comfortable to work in agency and on site client location as needed</p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<ul>\n<li>\n<p>Ability to estimate work effort, plan in collaboration with the program team and ensure consistent velocity of work output<br><br></p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p><strong>Qualifications &amp; Characteristics</strong></p>\n<ul>\n<li>\n<p>You inspire and develop designers, giving constructive feedback and encouraging growth.</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Balance creative ambition with constraints, adapt quickly to changing briefs, and find smart solutions.</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Present ideas and design decisions with clarity and confidence to diverse stakeholders.</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Thrive in fast-paced environments, manage pressure, and maintain quality under tight timelines.</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>You believe in collaboration, respect diverse perspectives, and build camaraderie across creative, strategy, and production teams</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Possess great written and verbal communication skills</p>\n</li>\n</ul><div><p><span>We believe great work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach including required in-office days. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.</span></p>\n<p><span>AKQA is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.</span></p>\n<hr>\n<p><span></span></p>\n<p><strong><span data-olk-copy-source=\"MessageBody\">About WPP</span></strong></p>\n<p>WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. We unite&nbsp;cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel&nbsp;in a single company –&nbsp;powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open,&nbsp;to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. For more information, visit&nbsp;<a href=\"http://wpp.com/\" data-auth=\"NotApplicable\" data-linkindex=\"0\">WPP.com</a>.</p>\n<p><span></span></p></div>

title: Interactive Visual Designer
description: <p>At AKQA (part of WPP) we believe in the imaginative application of art and science to create beautiful ideas, products and services. With an entrepreneurial and collaborative approach, we design adaptive digital services and applications that capture the imagination, delivering impressive returns for our clients.</p>\n<p>As an Interactive Visual Designer in the design team, you will shape user experiences for our client’s digital channels, ensuring that each interaction is intuitive, engaging, and reflects design excellence. You will design vibrant, usable, and technically sound visual experiences across digital touchpoints for our clients. You will focus on the intersection of motion, interface, and visual storytelling — enhancing usability through aesthetic and interactive visual designs. You will collaborate closely with UX, product, engineering, and design teams to bring ideas and concepts to life while maintaining visual and brand integrity.</p>\n<p>Your work will ensure that every screen, transition, micro-interaction, and visual asset aligns with established design ethos, elevating the creative vision and brand experience.</p>\n<p>Location - Mumbai&nbsp;</p>\n<p><strong>Role Requirements</strong></p>\n<ul>\n<li>\n<p>6-8 years’ experience in visual, interactive, or UI design roles within agency, product, or digital studio environments</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Strong portfolio demonstrating interactive and/or motion-focused design across web &amp; mobile form factors</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Proficiency in design and prototyping tools (Figma, Sketch, Adobe XD, Principle, Framer, etc.)</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Solid grounding in typography, layout, color, iconography, and visual hierarchy</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Experience designing responsive/ adaptive interfaces across multiple platforms</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Familiarity with design systems, component libraries, and style guides</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Basic understanding of front-end (HTML, CSS, JS) for developer collaboration</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) in Graphic Design, Interaction Design, Visual Communication, or related field</p>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p><strong>Qualifications and Characteristics</strong></p>\n<ul>\n<li>\n<p>Strong visual eye: balance, polish, and design integrity</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Ability to craft smooth, thoughtful interactions and motion design</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Detail-oriented with a passion for pixel perfection and consistency</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Skilled at usability evaluation, prototyping, and iteration</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Comfortable working in a multi disciplinary set up and at client locations</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Curious and experimental — actively exploring trends, tools, and new techniques</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Resilient in fast-paced environments, adaptable to ambiguity and shifting priorities</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Proactive mindset, ownership mentality, and accountability from ideation to delivery</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p>Possess great written and verbal communication skills</p>\n</li>\n</ul><div><p><span>We believe great work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. title: Creative Director
description: <div>\n<div>\n<div>\n<div>\n<div>\n<div>At AKQA, we believe in the imaginative application of art, technology, and human insight to create meaningful experiences that shape the future. With an entrepreneurial and experimental mindset, we design ideas, products, and environments that don’t just communicate, they transform how people live, feel, and connect.</div>\n<div><br>As a Creative Director, Art, you will guide the visual direction across campaigns, platforms, identity expressions, film, social content, and integrated communication. You will lead multidisciplinary teams to develop world-class ideas, elevate craft, and ensure every visual output feels intentional, distinctive, and culturally resonant.</div>\n<div><br>You have a strong point of view, a refined sense of taste, and a deep understanding of how design and storytelling create meaning. You lead through inspiration, clarity, and creative pressure.<br>At AKQA you will have access to world-renowned thought leadership. You will work in an environment which fosters a meritocratic culture and encourages ownership, and you will be surrounded by some of the brightest minds in their fields.<br><br>ROLE REQUIREMENTS</div>\n<ul>\n<li>Direct with aesthetic conviction. You define the visual heartbeat of the idea, the mood, the palette, the rhythm, the cinematic energy, and you pull teams toward a stronger, sharper story through visuals.</li>\n<li>Lead with creative hunger. Drive concepts, visuals, and design systems with the energy of a maker and the ambition of a leader, pushing every idea to be braver, sharper, and more culturally relevant.</li>\n<li>Be a storyteller with taste. Shape narratives through visuals, composition, rhythm, and emotion, not just decoration. Every frame, layout, and edit must convey intention, story, and meaning.</li>\n<li>Craft with obsession. Whether it’s a platform identity, a film, a digital experience, a product UI, or a prototype, treat details like a signature. Your work should feel unmistakably world-class.</li>\n<li>Think beyond the screen. Build ideas that live as acts, installations, products, content ecosystems, and tech-enabled experiences, not just campaigns. Bring innovation and experimentation into the room naturally.</li>\n<li>Make ideas PR-able by design. Create visuals and concepts that earn cultural attention, not just meet a brief. Understand what travels regionally and globally, and design for impact.</li>\n<li>Elevate the room. Bring energy, references, inspiration, and ideas that push the team forward. Mentor juniors, shape thinking, and help raise the studio’s creative temperature.</li>\n<li>Challenge the brief, respectfully. Ask the questions others don’t. Find the tension, expose the opportunity, and push for the version of the work that truly moves people.</li>\n<li>Guard the quality. Be accountable for every output. You teach taste, discipline, and ambition. You show juniors what “good” looks like, and what “great” could be with two more pushes.</li>\n<li>Guard the quality with obsession. title: Creative Director (Tokyo)
description: <p>As a Creative Director and the leader of the AKQA Tokyo (part of WPP) studio’s Creative department, you will create and inspire work that is distinctive, unfamiliar, effective and truly remarkable to make a difference to consumers’ behavior through ideas, creativity and the use of interactive media. Work with other AKQA network creatives collaboratively on joint initiatives.</li>\n<li>Work closely with the strategy teams to shape briefs that empower our teams to deliver work that pushes our client's brand to the forefront of their global audience's minds</li>\n<li>&nbsp;</li>\n</ul>\n<p><strong>Key Responsibilities</strong></p>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Creative briefs </strong>\n<ul>\n<li>Contributes to strategic development of a well-defined creative brief and any additional briefing documents, speaking with the client directly when necessary. Understands how to respond to a brief.</li>\n</ul>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Idea generation</strong>\n<ul>\n<li>Leads by example to consistently inspire creative colleagues to originate world- class, pioneering ideas that conceptualize world-class solutions. Generates useful, usable ideas across all existing and emerging digital platforms. Continually develops practices and processes to inspire, innovate and harness new ways of thinking.</li>\n</ul>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>◊</strong> <strong>New business development </strong>\n<ul>\n<li>Creatively leads new business pitches. Responsible for crisp ideas and creative workflow; effective and dynamic storyteller for oral pitch presentation in both Japanese &amp; English.</li>\n</ul>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Creative craft skills</strong>\n<ul>\n<li>Selects most appropriate supplier for required task. Successfully leads multiple suppliers, partners and clients to ensure outsourced projects are creatively delivered on time, within budget and to the highest standards. Helps to ensure AKQA continues to create unique and inspirational solutions.</li>\n</ul>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Team management</strong>\n<ul>\n<li>Manages creatives as direct reports. title: Associate Creative Director (Tokyo)
description: <p>AKQA (part of WPP) is a creative agency that transforms strategic insights into beautiful concepts through art and craftsmanship, guiding clients toward a better future.</p>\n<p><br>As an Associate Creative Director, you will be expected to go beyond the brief—developing strategic approaches based on consumer insight, generating bold and innovative ideas, and presenting them as compelling narratives to clients.<br><br></p>\n<p>At AKQA, our work extends far beyond advertising. From building brand and CX visions to designing digital and physical experiences, our projects span a wide range of disciplines. While leveraging your creative expertise built through past experience, you will also be expected to cross boundaries and take on new challenges in unexplored domains.<br><br></p>\n<p>In particular, AKQA Tokyo is on a mission to grow our team and become Japan’s leading creative agency—one that creates a better future for brands through transformative brand experiences that go beyond traditional marketing. We are looking for passionate, ambitious new talent to join us in this journey.</p>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<p>Role Requirements</p>\n<ul>\n<li>Deliver and inspire award-winning work from both yourself and others who work with you, influencing where appropriate a dramatic change through ideas, creativity and the use of interactive media.</li>\n<li>Lead, direct and manage a cross-disciplinary and culturally diverse set of talents, both internal and external to AKQA to deliver successful solutions and high-quality works to clients on time.</li>\n<li>Create ground-breaking work that positively impacts our clients, our teams and the world.</li>\n<li>Foster and line-manage creatives that are eager to grow their craft in art direction, copywriting or design.</li>\n<li>Learn new skills and stay abreast of technical and digital design developments, and successfully apply them to work practices to help improve processes and procedures.</li>\n<li>Work with other AKQA network creatives collaboratively on joint initiatives.</li>\n<li>Work closely with the strategy teams to shape briefs that empower our teams to deliver work that pushes our client's brand to the forefront of their global audience's minds.</li>\n<li>Extensive hands-on experience in client work within Japan, with the ability to communicate effectively with both team members and clients in both Japanese and English.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<p>Key Responsibilities<br><br></p>\n<ul>\n<li>Creative briefs</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Contributes to strategic development of a well-defined creative brief and any additional briefing documents, speaking with the client directly when necessary. Listens to and responds to a brief, understanding where/if there are gaps. Knows what questions to ask during the briefing session in order to act upon it. Understands how to respond to a brief.</p>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li>Idea generation<br>Leads by example to consistently inspire creative colleagues to originate world- class, pioneering ideas that conceptualize world-class solutions. Generates useful, usable ideas across all existing and emerging digital platforms. Continually develops practices and processes to inspire, innovate and harness new ways of thinking.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li>New business development<br>Creatively leads new business pitches. Responsible for crisp ideas and creative workflow; effective and dynamic storyteller for oral pitch presentation.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li>Creative craft skills<br>Selects the most appropriate supplier for the required task. Successfully leads multiple suppliers, partners and clients to ensure outsourced projects are creatively delivered on time, within budget and to the highest standards. Helps to ensure AKQA continues to create unique and inspirational solutions.<br><br></li>\n<li>Stakeholder management<br>Successfully manages multiple suppliers, partners and customers to ensure the best outcome for both customer and AKQA. Keeps stakeholders informed of project progress at all times and manages expectations proactively.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li>Client Leadership<br>Understand client business goals and ambitions. Takes briefs from clients and successfully creates stories that communicate client’s needs and ambitions to the consumers.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<p>&nbsp;</p><div><p><span>We believe great work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach including required in-office days. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.</span></p>\n<p><span>AKQA is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.</span></p>\n<hr>\n<p><span></span></p>\n<p><strong><span data-olk-copy-source=\"MessageBody\">About WPP</span></strong></p>\n<p>WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. We unite&nbsp;cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel&nbsp;in a single company –&nbsp;powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open,&nbsp;to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. For more information, visit&nbsp;<a href=\"http://wpp.com/\" data-auth=\"NotApplicable\" data-linkindex=\"0\">WPP.com</a>.</p>\n<p><span></span></p></div>

title: Senior User Experience (UX) Designer
description: <p>Experience Designers at AKQA (part of WPP) concept, craft and deliver interfaces that make technology more natural and enjoyable to use. In collaboration with strategists, researchers and technologists, our UI and UX designers take a human-centred approach to create beautiful and meaningful brand experiences.</p>\n<p>As a Senior UX Designer you will play an essential role balancing business, customer and technical requirements to craft blueprints for solutions that delight clients and users.</p>\n<p>As part of the AKQA team in Tokyo, you will work in a dynamic, globally-minded environment surrounded by some of the brightest minds in their fields. Together you will create world-class experiences for both Japanese and overseas markets.</p>\n<p><strong>ROLE REQUIREMENTS</strong></p>\n<ul>\n<li>Plan and execute core UX/CX deliverables including heuristic and IA audits, ecosystem maps, personas, customer journeys, service blueprints, and wireframes</li>\n<li>Collaborate across disciplines in both agile and waterfall processes to quickly deliver impactful solutions</li>\n<li>Present to business stakeholders to help them make informed decisions that strengthen their business</li>\n<li>Keep on top of best practices and trends to ensure we are at the forefront of the industry</li>\n<li>Lead and inspire, sharing your knowledge with peers and mentoring mid/junior designers<br><br></li>\n</ul>\n<p><strong>QUALITIES AND CHARACTERISTICS</strong></p>\n<ul>\n<li>A compelling portfolio demonstrating outstanding UX design, with a desire to become even better</li>\n<li>Excellent communication skills, with the ability to make complex topics understandable, and present logically</li>\n<li>Excellent writing and editing skills, experienced in crafting effective, on-brand micro copy</li>\n<li>Mastery of whiteboard and prototyping software such as Miro and Figma</li>\n<li>A “can-do” attitude, eager to embrace big challenges</li>\n<li>Attention to detail and a passion for getting to the root of a problem</li>\n<li>Ability to thrive in a global team, with an open mind to fresh perspectives</li>\n<li>Proven experience working in a Senior UX Design role (agency or in-house)</li>\n<li>A degree in Interaction Design, Information Architecture or other design-related field is preferred</li>\n<li>Fluency in Japanese at N1 or native level is required</li>\n<li>Conversational or higher English ability is preferred but not required</li>\n</ul>\n<p>#LI-DNI</p><div><p><span>We believe great work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach including required in-office days. title: Associate Design Director (XD) (UKA) (Tokyo)
description: <p>At AKQA (part of WPP) we believe in the imaginative application of art and science to create beautiful ideas, products and services. With an entrepreneurial and collaborative approach, we design adaptive digital services and experiences that capture the imagination, delivering impressive returns for our clients.&nbsp;</p>\n<p>The Associate Design Director at AKQA will approach every brief with fresh eyes. Avoiding familiar solutions, the candidate will create and inspire work that is distinctive, effective and truly remarkable. They lead by example to consistently inspire creative colleagues to create world-class, pioneering ideas that conceptualise world-class solutions, while acting as a strategic and conceptual leader on projects. Associate Design Directors at AKQA display a cohesive understanding and can execute the principles that make innovative and effective digital communications, interface design, and interaction design.</p>\n<p>We exist to create the future with our clients and that partnership is the basis of our practice. We work collaboratively across client teams to ensure each experience is of the highest quality and has a measurable impact on the customer and business alike.</p>\n<p>As part of the AKQA team in Tokyo, you will work in a dynamic, globally-minded environment surrounded by some of the brightest minds in their fields. Together you will create world-class experiences for both Japanese and overseas markets.</p>\n<p><strong>ROLE REQUIREMENTS</strong></p>\n<ul>\n<li>Sets creative direction throughout all phases of project work from discovery to launch and beyond with ongoing optimization</li>\n<li>Acts as a creative lead for one or more accounts, managing multiple tracks of work across a variety of inspire, innovate, and harness new ways of thinking</li>\n<li>Inspires and leads by example, setting professional standards for the team and the department</li>\n<li>Actively trains, mentors, and manages junior to mid-level creatives and designers.</li>\n<li>Responsible for managing and guiding junior to mid-level creatives’ careers within AKQA (including regular one-on-one check-ins), writing and delivering annual performance reviews, and taking responsibility for on-boarding new employees and guiding them through a successful tenure at AKQA</li>\n<li>Creatively participates in new business pitches – i.e. responsible for ideas and creative work, and competently presents pitches</li>\n<li>Be able to guide but also prepared to be hands on with exceptional experience in industry design tools<br><br></li>\n</ul>\n<p><strong>QUALITIES AND CHARACTERISTICS</strong></p>\n<ul>\n<li>A minimum of 7 years’ experience in the creative industry</li>\n<li>Native or fluent Japanese reading working knowledge of user interface, application, mobile and responsive design</li>\n<li>Exceptional communication skills: Verbal, written, interpersonal and inter-departmental</li>\n<li>Superb title: Motion Designer
description: <p>Creatives at AKQA apply artistry and craftsmanship to strategic insight, creating and bringing to life beautiful and technologically innovative ideas that move the world. From a variety of backgrounds and disciplines, our creatives are innately curious and strive to transform client challenges into beautiful, cutting-edge solutions.</p>\n<p>As a Motion Designer at our Berlin studio, you’ll be instrumental in building impactful brand motion systems, animating engaging campaigns, and crafting captivating experiences across digital products, .com platforms, and apps. With strong conceptual and strategic foundations, you’ll work alongside art directors, designers, and our broader creative team to transform ideas into captivating motion graphics and animations that will elevate our clients’ brand stories across every touchpoint. You’re a visionary designer who sees motion as an art form that plays a key role in bringing a brand to life across the entire customer journey.</p>\n<p>As part of the AKQA team, you will work in an environment that fosters a dynamic and inclusive culture, enabled by our multidisciplinary approach and supported by some of the brightest minds in their field. You will report to a Creative Director and will have a close, collaborative relationship with the other Creative, Design, and Experience leads across the studio.</p>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<p><strong>ROLE REQUIREMENTS</strong></p>\n<ul data-start=\"258\" data-end=\"1800\">\n<li data-start=\"258\" data-end=\"481\">Lead the creation of distinctive motion design systems for brand identities, marketing campaigns, digital products (.com platforms, apps), and communications—grounded in strong conceptual thinking and strategic insight.</li>\n<li data-start=\"482\" data-end=\"700\">Design and deliver high-quality 2D and 3D motion, UI animations, and brand motion principles across a wide range of outputs, including brand films, campaign sizzles, social content, and immersive visual narratives.</li>\n<li data-start=\"701\" data-end=\"876\">Collaborate closely with creative directors, art directors, brand and experience designers, and client teams to bring ambitious ideas to life and push creative boundaries.</li>\n<li data-start=\"877\" data-end=\"1050\">Stay at the forefront of motion and digital design trends, combining tools, techniques, and emerging technologies to create work that shapes culture and elevates brands.</li>\n<li data-start=\"1051\" data-end=\"1224\">Confidently move between defining scalable motion systems, animating high-impact campaigns, and enhancing digital product experiences with thoughtful, expressive motion.</li>\n<li data-start=\"1225\" data-end=\"1356\">Create clear, compelling client-facing presentations that communicate vision, conceptual rationale, and executional guidelines.</li>\n<li data-start=\"1357\" data-end=\"1481\">Translate brand and experience strategies into innovative motion solutions for complex, multi-channel design challenges.</li>\n<li data-start=\"1482\" data-end=\"1629\">Mentor and support mid-level and junior designers, helping raise the overall quality and ambition of motion work across film, 2D/3D, and video.</li>\n<li data-start=\"1630\" data-end=\"1800\">Lead the motion discipline on client projects, owning deliverables, maintaining momentum, and ensuring work meets timelines and AKQA’s standards of creative excellence.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<p>QUALITIES AND CHARACTERISTICS</p>\n<ul data-start=\"1839\" data-end=\"2713\">\n<li data-start=\"1839\" data-end=\"2002\">A standout portfolio demonstrating depth and range across brand identity, integrated campaigns, communications, and digital product experiences (.com and app).</li>\n<li data-start=\"2003\" data-end=\"2060\">5+ years of professional experience in motion design.</li>\n<li data-start=\"2061\" data-end=\"2248\">Advanced expertise in After Effects and strong proficiency with relevant design tools, paired with a curiosity for emerging platforms and techniques such as TouchDesigner and Cavalry.</li>\n<li data-start=\"2249\" data-end=\"2381\">A culturally curious mindset, with a strong awareness of design, technology, and visual culture—and an instinct for what’s next.</li>\n<li data-start=\"2382\" data-end=\"2481\">Experience with Cinema 4D, Houdini, or other 3D tools is a plus, though not a core requirement.</li>\n<li data-start=\"2482\" data-end=\"2620\">A conceptual thinker who approaches motion with intention—always understanding the “why” behind every movement, system, and execution.</li>\n<li data-start=\"2621\" data-end=\"2713\">A self-driven learner with a relentless desire to grow, experiment, and refine your craft.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Explore our extensive range of benefits <a href=\"https://www.akqa.com/studios/berlin/benefits/\">here</a></p><div><p><span>We believe great work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach including required in-office days. title: Creative Director
description: <div>\n<div>\n<div>\n<div>\n<div>\n<div>At AKQA, we believe in the imaginative application of art, technology, and human insight to create meaningful experiences that shape the future. With an entrepreneurial and experimental mindset, we design ideas, products, and environments that don’t just communicate, they transform how people live, feel, and connect.</div>\n<div><br>As a Creative Director, Art, you will guide the visual direction across campaigns, platforms, identity expressions, film, social content, and integrated communication. You will lead multidisciplinary teams to develop world-class ideas, elevate craft, and ensure every visual output feels intentional, distinctive, and culturally resonant.</div>\n<div><br>You have a strong point of view, a refined sense of taste, and a deep understanding of how design and storytelling create meaning. You lead through inspiration, clarity, and creative pressure.<br>At AKQA you will have access to world-renowned thought leadership. You will work in an environment which fosters a meritocratic culture and encourages ownership, and you will be surrounded by some of the brightest minds in their fields.<br><br>ROLE REQUIREMENTS</div>\n<ul>\n<li>Direct with aesthetic conviction. You define the visual heartbeat of the idea, the mood, the palette, the rhythm, the cinematic energy, and you pull teams toward a stronger, sharper story through visuals.</li>\n<li>Lead with creative hunger. Drive concepts, visuals, and design systems with the energy of a maker and the ambition of a leader, pushing every idea to be braver, sharper, and more culturally relevant.</li>\n<li>Be a storyteller with taste. Shape narratives through visuals, composition, rhythm, and emotion, not just decoration. Every frame, layout, and edit must convey intention, story, and meaning.</li>\n<li>Craft with obsession. Whether it’s a platform identity, a film, a digital experience, a product UI, or a prototype, treat details like a signature. Your work should feel unmistakably world-class.</li>\n<li>Think beyond the screen. Build ideas that live as acts, installations, products, content ecosystems, and tech-enabled experiences, not just campaigns. Bring innovation and experimentation into the room naturally.</li>\n<li>Make ideas PR-able by design. Create visuals and concepts that earn cultural attention, not just meet a brief. Understand what travels regionally and globally, and design for impact.</li>\n<li>Elevate the room. Bring energy, references, inspiration, and ideas that push the team forward. Mentor juniors, shape thinking, and help raise the studio’s creative temperature.</li>\n<li>Challenge the brief, respectfully. Ask the questions others don’t. Find the tension, expose the opportunity, and push for the version of the work that truly moves people.</li>\n<li>Guard the quality. Be accountable for every output. You teach taste, discipline, and ambition. You show juniors what “good” looks like, and what “great” could be with two more pushes.</li>\n<li>Guard the quality with obsession. Stay attuned to visual culture, design movements, platform behaviours, emerging tools, and evolving aesthetics.</li>\n</ul>\n<div><br><br>QUALITIES AND CHARACTERISTICS</div>\n<ul>\n<li>A portfolio demonstrating world-class art direction, visual storytelling, and integrated campaign craft and ideation.</li>\n<li>Experience leading multidisciplinary creative teams in communication-based environments.</li>\n<li>Strong sense of taste, composition, and storytelling through visuals.</li>\n<li>Deep cultural awareness, curiosity, and an experimental mindset</li>\n<li>Passion for pushing boundaries in mediums, formats, platforms, and ways of experiencing ideas.</li>\n<li>Confident communicator, clear, inspiring, and articulate.</li>\n<li>Passion for innovation, experimentation, and continually elevating craft.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>We are proactively engaging with talent for a prospective role in preparation for future business needs.</p>\n</div>\n<div>\n<div>Explore our extensive range of benefits&nbsp;<a href=\"https://www.akqa.com/studios/mena/benefits/\"><strong>here.</strong></a></div>\n</div>\n</div>\n<div>&nbsp;</div>\n<div>&nbsp;</div>\n</div>\n<div>\n<div>&nbsp;</div>\n</div>\n</div>\n</div><div><p><span>We believe great work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. title: Creative Director (Tokyo)
description: <p>As a Creative Director and the leader of the AKQA Tokyo (part of WPP) studio’s Creative department, you will create and inspire work that is distinctive, unfamiliar, effective and truly remarkable to make a difference to consumers’ behavior through ideas, creativity and the use of interactive media. You will inspire and lead a team to raise their bar even higher, mentoring a team, leading by example and championing the discipline in the wider agency and beyond.&nbsp;</p>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<p><strong>Role Requirements</strong></p>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li>Deliver and inspire award-winning work from both yourself and others who work with you, influencing where appropriate a dramatic change through ideas, creativity and the use of interactive media.</li>\n<li>Lead, direct and manage cross-disciplinary and culturally diverse set of talents, both internal and external to AKQA to deliver successful solutions and high-quality works to clients on time.</li>\n<li>Create ground-breaking work that positively impacts our clients, our teams and the world.</li>\n<li>Foster and line-manage creatives that are eager to grow their craft in art direction, copywriting or design.</li>\n<li>High level of accuracy and attention to detail.</li>\n<li>Learn new skills and stay abreast of technical and digital design developments, and successfully apply them to work practices to help improve processes and procedures.</li>\n<li>Act as the creative representative of the Tokyo studio within and outside AKQA network, in Japanese and in English. Work with other AKQA network creatives collaboratively on joint initiatives.</li>\n<li>Work closely with the strategy teams to shape briefs that empower our teams to deliver work that pushes our client's brand to the forefront of their global audience's minds</li>\n<li>&nbsp;</li>\n</ul>\n<p><strong>Key Responsibilities</strong></p>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Creative briefs </strong>\n<ul>\n<li>Contributes to strategic development of a well-defined creative brief and any additional briefing documents, speaking with the client directly when necessary. Listens to and responds to a brief, understanding where/if there are gaps. Knows what questions to ask during the briefing session in order to act upon it. Understands how to respond to a brief.</li>\n</ul>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Idea generation</strong>\n<ul>\n<li>Leads by example to consistently inspire creative colleagues to originate world- class, pioneering ideas that conceptualize world-class solutions. Generates useful, usable ideas across all existing and emerging digital platforms. Continually develops practices and processes to inspire, innovate and harness new ways of thinking.</li>\n</ul>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>◊</strong> <strong>New business development </strong>\n<ul>\n<li>Creatively leads new business pitches. Responsible for crisp ideas and creative workflow; effective and dynamic storyteller for oral pitch presentation in both Japanese &amp; English.</li>\n</ul>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Creative craft skills</strong>\n<ul>\n<li>Selects most appropriate supplier for required task. Successfully leads multiple suppliers, partners and clients to ensure outsourced projects are creatively delivered on time, within budget and to the highest standards. Helps to ensure AKQA continues to create unique and inspirational solutions.</li>\n</ul>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Team management</strong>\n<ul>\n<li>Manages creatives as direct reports. Provides an international and supportive working environment, based on openness, trust and regular constructive feedback on performance. Sets clear and achievable goals. Takes immediate action to address performance/conduct issues. Assesses a broad range of tasks and determines which team member should manage to ensure delivery to the highest standard. Is an ambassador for AKQA and its values at all times.</li>\n<li>Demonstrates creative success in leading collaborative multidisciplinary teams, including project planning, to winning a number of industry recognized awards.</li>\n</ul>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Stakeholder management</strong>\n<ul>\n<li>Successfully manages multiple suppliers, partners and customers to ensure the best outcome for both customer and AKQA. Keeps stakeholders informed of project progress at all times and manages expectations proactively.</li>\n</ul>\n</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Client Leadership</strong>\n<ul>\n<li>Understand client business goals and ambitions. title: Associate Creative Director (Tokyo)
description: <p>AKQA (part of WPP) is a creative agency that transforms strategic insights into beautiful concepts through art and craftsmanship, guiding clients toward a better future.</p>\n<p><br>As an Associate Creative Director, you will be expected to go beyond the brief—developing strategic approaches based on consumer insight, generating bold and innovative ideas, and presenting them as compelling narratives to clients.<br><br></p>\n<p>At AKQA, our work extends far beyond advertising. From building brand and CX visions to designing digital and physical experiences, our projects span a wide range of disciplines. While leveraging your creative expertise built through past experience, you will also be expected to cross boundaries and take on new challenges in unexplored domains.<br><br></p>\n<p>In particular, AKQA Tokyo is on a mission to grow our team and become Japan’s leading creative agency—one that creates a better future for brands through transformative brand experiences that go beyond traditional marketing. We are looking for passionate, ambitious new talent to join us in this journey.</p>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<p>Role Requirements</p>\n<ul>\n<li>Deliver and inspire award-winning work from both yourself and others who work with you, influencing where appropriate a dramatic change through ideas, creativity and the use of interactive media.</li>\n<li>Lead, direct and manage a cross-disciplinary and culturally diverse set of talents, both internal and external to AKQA to deliver successful solutions and high-quality works to clients on time.</li>\n<li>Create ground-breaking work that positively impacts our clients, our teams and the world.</li>\n<li>Foster and line-manage creatives that are eager to grow their craft in art direction, copywriting or design.</li>\n<li>Learn new skills and stay abreast of technical and digital design developments, and successfully apply them to work practices to help improve processes and procedures.</li>\n<li>Work with other AKQA network creatives collaboratively on joint initiatives.</li>\n<li>Work closely with the strategy teams to shape briefs that empower our teams to deliver work that pushes our client's brand to the forefront of their global audience's minds.</li>\n<li>Extensive hands-on experience in client work within Japan, with the ability to communicate effectively with both team members and clients in both Japanese and English.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<p>Key Responsibilities<br><br></p>\n<ul>\n<li>Creative briefs</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Contributes to strategic development of a well-defined creative brief and any additional briefing documents, speaking with the client directly when necessary. Listens to and responds to a brief, understanding where/if there are gaps. Knows what questions to ask during the briefing session in order to act upon it. Understands how to respond to a brief.</p>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li>Idea generation<br>Leads by example to consistently inspire creative colleagues to originate world- class, pioneering ideas that conceptualize world-class solutions. Generates useful, usable ideas across all existing and emerging digital platforms. Continually develops practices and processes to inspire, innovate and harness new ways of thinking.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li>New business development<br>Creatively leads new business pitches. Responsible for crisp ideas and creative workflow; effective and dynamic storyteller for oral pitch presentation.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li>Creative craft skills<br>Selects the most appropriate supplier for the required task. Successfully leads multiple suppliers, partners and clients to ensure outsourced projects are creatively delivered on time, within budget and to the highest standards. Helps to ensure AKQA continues to create unique and inspirational solutions.<br><br></li>\n<li>Stakeholder management<br>Successfully manages multiple suppliers, partners and customers to ensure the best outcome for both customer and AKQA. Keeps stakeholders informed of project progress at all times and manages expectations proactively.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<ul>\n<li>Client Leadership<br>Understand client business goals and ambitions. Takes briefs from clients and successfully creates stories that communicate client’s needs and ambitions to the consumers.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<p>&nbsp;</p><div><p><span>We believe great work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach including required in-office days. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.</span></p>\n<p><span>AKQA is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.</span></p>\n<hr>\n<p><span></span></p>\n<p><strong><span data-olk-copy-source=\"MessageBody\">About WPP</span></strong></p>\n<p>WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. We unite&nbsp;cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel&nbsp;in a single company –&nbsp;powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open,&nbsp;to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. For more information, visit&nbsp;<a href=\"http://wpp.com/\" data-auth=\"NotApplicable\" data-linkindex=\"0\">WPP.com</a>.</p>\n<p><span></span></p></div>

title: Senior User Experience (UX) Designer
description: <p>Experience Designers at AKQA (part of WPP) concept, craft and deliver interfaces that make technology more natural and enjoyable to use. In collaboration with strategists, researchers and technologists, our UI and UX designers take a human-centred approach to create beautiful and meaningful brand experiences.</p>\n<p>As a Senior UX Designer you will play an essential role balancing business, customer and technical requirements to craft blueprints for solutions that delight clients and users.</p>\n<p>As part of the AKQA team in Tokyo, you will work in a dynamic, globally-minded environment surrounded by some of the brightest minds in their fields. Together you will create world-class experiences for both Japanese and overseas markets.</p>\n<p><strong>ROLE REQUIREMENTS</strong></p>\n<ul>\n<li>Plan and execute core UX/CX deliverables including heuristic and IA audits, ecosystem maps, personas, customer journeys, service blueprints, and wireframes</li>\n<li>Collaborate across disciplines in both agile and waterfall processes to quickly deliver impactful solutions</li>\n<li>Present to business stakeholders to help them make informed decisions that strengthen their business</li>\n<li>Keep on top of best practices and trends to ensure we are at the forefront of the industry</li>\n<li>Lead and inspire, sharing your knowledge with peers and mentoring mid/junior designers<br><br></li>\n</ul>\n<p><strong>QUALITIES AND CHARACTERISTICS</strong></p>\n<ul>\n<li>A compelling portfolio demonstrating outstanding UX design, with a desire to become even better</li>\n<li>Excellent communication skills, with the ability to make complex topics understandable, and present logically</li>\n<li>Excellent writing and editing skills, experienced in crafting effective, on-brand micro copy</li>\n<li>Mastery of whiteboard and prototyping software such as Miro and Figma</li>\n<li>A “can-do” attitude, eager to embrace big challenges</li>\n<li>Attention to detail and a passion for getting to the root of a problem</li>\n<li>Ability to thrive in a global team, with an open mind to fresh perspectives</li>\n<li>Proven experience working in a Senior UX Design role (agency or in-house)</li>\n<li>A degree in Interaction Design, Information Architecture or other design-related field is preferred</li>\n<li>Fluency in Japanese at N1 or native level is required</li>\n<li>Conversational or higher English ability is preferred but not required</li>\n</ul>\n<p>#LI-DNI</p><div><p><span>We believe great work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach including required in-office days. title: Associate Design Director (XD) (UKA) (Tokyo)
description: <p>At AKQA (part of WPP) we believe in the imaginative application of art and science to create beautiful ideas, products and services. With an entrepreneurial and collaborative approach, we design adaptive digital services and experiences that capture the imagination, delivering impressive returns for our clients.&nbsp;</p>\n<p>The Associate Design Director at AKQA will approach every brief with fresh eyes. Avoiding familiar solutions, the candidate will create and inspire work that is distinctive, effective and truly remarkable. They lead by example to consistently inspire creative colleagues to create world-class, pioneering ideas that conceptualise world-class solutions, while acting as a strategic and conceptual leader on projects. Associate Design Directors at AKQA display a cohesive understanding and can execute the principles that make innovative and effective digital communications, interface design, and interaction design.</p>\n<p>We exist to create the future with our clients and that partnership is the basis of our practice. We work collaboratively across client teams to ensure each experience is of the highest quality and has a measurable impact on the customer and business alike.</p>\n<p>As part of the AKQA team in Tokyo, you will work in a dynamic, globally-minded environment surrounded by some of the brightest minds in their fields. Together you will create world-class experiences for both Japanese and overseas markets.</p>\n<p><strong>ROLE REQUIREMENTS</strong></p>\n<ul>\n<li>Sets creative direction throughout all phases of project work from discovery to launch and beyond with ongoing optimization</li>\n<li>Acts as a creative lead for one or more accounts, managing multiple tracks of work across a variety of
