title: Video Editor, A Touch More & Bird's Eye View
description: Back to jobsVideo Editor, A Touch More & Bird's Eye View New York, New York The Vox Media Podcast Network is looking for a Video Editor to lead the visual identity and post-production for “A Touch More” and “Bird’s Eye View,” two flagship women’s sports and lifestyle podcasts. You will own the video editing workflow for full-length episodes and collaborate on creating compelling short-form content optimized for social platforms. The ideal candidate is a meticulous and fast technical editor who understands how podcasts move through the modern media ecosystem. You should have a strong sense of visual style and a portfolio that showcases conceptually bold work. WHAT YOU’LL DO Execute Two Weekly Series: Lead the end-to-end editing and visual post-production for one to two full-length podcast episodes per week, distributed on YouTube and other video platforms, and support the creation of social media clips. Develop Visual Identity: Translate the personality and pacing of our hosts into a distinct and elevated podcast aesthetic. Enhance Engagement: Design and integrate dynamic motion graphics, lower thirds, captions, and visual cues that enhance the narrative and tone. Technical Polish: Ensure consistent branding through the expert use of color, typography, and layout. Creative Problem Solving: Address mixed-location interviews (e.g., Zoom + studio footage, live event footage) with creativity, ensuring cohesion and polish regardless of source quality. Multi-Cam Management: Implement split-screen layouts and on-screen text to keep long-form conversations visually engaging and viewer-friendly. Collaborative Iteration: Work closely with hosts, producers, and showrunners to iterate quickly and deliver high-quality content on a strict weekly schedule. WHAT YOU'LL BRING Production Experience: Proven experience in video podcast production for a media brand, agency, or individual creator. Technical Fluency: Mastery of Adobe Premiere and a strong working knowledge motion design, graphic design, and audio editing programs Editorial Instinct: A keen eye for identifying the natural "flow" of a recording and a well-honed sense of pacing to keep viewers engaged throughout a full episode. Strong Portfolio: A body of work that showcases your ability to shape voice, tone, and rhythm through editing. Visual Storytelling: Comfort with multi-camera editing and techniques like overlays, animated inserts, and typography to emphasize clarity or humor. Efficiency and Flexibility: The ability to maintain high brand standards while working against the tight deadlines of a twice-weekly show with variable recording schedules. Platform Passion: Deep knowledge of the social media landscape and excitement about evolving podcasts into dynamic, multiplatform products. title: Executive Editor, Vox
description: Back to jobsExecutive Editor, VoxRemote (US Only)ApplyVox is seeking an Executive Editor who will bring a clear vision, original ideas, and a deep understanding of the political and policy landscape. This leader will define what Vox’s politics and policy journalism should look like in 2026 and beyond, ensuring our work serves audiences outside the political bubble and connects meaningfully with the next generation of audiences. You will guide and elevate Vox’s politics and policy coverage across formats (text and audio, and also collaborating with video), manage a high-performing team of reporters and editors, and oversee our flagship Today, Explained and Explain It to Me podcast teams. This role calls for someone who excels at identifying the political forces shaping people’s lives — and translating that into sharp, clear, memorable explanatory journalism. The ideal candidate is someone who can set a vision for politics coverage that focuses on serving everyday people across the US, rather than Washington insiders — meeting them where they are, answering their questions, and covering the stories that matter to them and impact their day-to-day lives. They should also be an innovator who is eager to experiment with new mediums. They will directly manage text and audio teams, but also collaborate closely with the video team to help ideate on video story ideas and formats that reach and serve audiences who consume their news through social platforms like YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok. They should be a strong, experienced people manager who is comfortable managing editors as well as reporters. They will be a key member of the newsroom leadership team, reporting to the Editor-in-Chief & Publisher. What You’ll Do Oversee three editorial teams in the newsroom: Politics, Policy, and Ideas; Today, Explained and Explain It to Me; and Even Better and Lifestyle. Shape vision and strategy for Vox’s political journalism in 2026 and the years ahead, centering audiences who feel overlooked or underserved by traditional political coverage. Direct daily, short-, and long-term editorial plans, managing the balance between bigger original pieces and shorter pieces that are responsive to news, and directing the team when it’s necessary to pivot from plans due to news. Develop coverage strategies that meet audience members where they are — including audiences newer to news, politically disengaged readers/listeners, and younger audiences with different expectations of how news should work. Ensure our journalism is accessible, relevant, and grounded in real-world questions and concerns, not just the narratives dominating political insiders’ conversations. Identify opportunities to grow reach and impact through new formats, collaborations, and storytelling approaches, and partner with other teams to execute on multiplatform ideas. Mentor team members at all levels, helping them develop ideas and angles, structure stories, and strengthen their reporting. Oversee editorial strategy and execution for Today, Explained and Explain It to Me, ensuring the shows meet Vox’s standards for clarity, depth, creativity, and broad accessibility. Strengthen collaboration between text, audio, and video teams to ensure cohesive, cross-platform political coverage. Work with senior leadership across teams to shape Vox’s priorities and coverage strategy around major political moments (elections, legislative cycles, global events, court decisions, etc.). title: Deputy Editor, Future Perfect
description: Back to jobsDeputy Editor, Future PerfectDC or NYCApplyVox Media employees thrive on innovation and change while ambitiously seeking to master their subject areas, hone their craft, and improve their communities. We help smart, talented, creative professionals make a meaningful impact in media, technology, and the world around them. Our Future Perfect section is an especially exciting place for that mission to come alive. Future Perfect covers the world through a particular prism: What are the best ways to do good in this world? Future Perfect strives to shine a light on neglected stories that have, or will have, a profound impact on the lives of billions of humans and animals. Vox is looking for a Deputy Editor for Future Perfect. This role is first and foremost a people-and-story job: you’ll be responsible for managing and editing Future Perfect’s three reporting fellows, helping them generate strong ideas, select the right stories, execute ambitious reporting, and publish clean, sharp copy on a consistent cadence. Because the fellows are relatively early career, this role also includes meaningful coaching and skills development, including planning occasional “brown bag” training sessions. In addition, the deputy editor will oversee and edit Future Perfect’s twice-weekly newsletter, and, in close partnership with the editor of Future Perfect, will lead the process of recruiting and selecting the next class of fellows who will start in the summer. The position will be a full-time, 16 month role. Among our key subject areas include: Emerging technology and artificial intelligence Philanthropy, altruism, and the institutions that shape giving Global health and global development Animal welfare and factory farming Catastrophic risks to humanity, including pandemics and nuclear war Other evidence-driven approaches to improving lives at scale WHAT YOU'LL DO Manage and edit three reporting fellows — soliciting and shaping ideas, selecting stories, guiding reporting plans, editing drafts, and supporting fellows’ growth as journalists at Vox. This includes helping fellows build strong habits around sourcing, structure, evidence, fairness, and voice, while also meeting publishing goals. Provide journalism training and coaching for early-career reporters, including designing occasional brown-bag sessions (e.g., story framing, interviewing, working with studies/data, reporting ethics, writing with clarity and voice, and navigating edits). Oversee the recruitment and selection of the next class of three reporting fellows, beginning in the spring. This includes outreach to potential candidates, managing and winnowing applications, designing and evaluating edit tests, coordinating interview loops, and helping make final selections in conjunction with the editor of Future Perfect. Oversee and edit Future Perfect’s twice-weekly newsletter, including shaping lineups, editing copy, sharpening voice and structure, and ensuring the newsletter reliably reflects the best of Future Perfect’s mission and journalism. Support day-to-day editorial operations as needed: helping maintain story calendars, identifying gaps and opportunities in coverage, coordinating with partners across Vox (copy desk, art, audience, audio/video), and stepping in on additional editing and management duties to keep the section running smoothly. WHAT YOU'LL BRING At least 3 years of full-time editing experience, ideally in a fast-paced digital newsroom environment; management experience is a major plus. A clear point of view on how to coach and develop writers, especially early-career reporters, with a willingness to give direct feedback while building trust and momentum. Strong story judgment and editing range — you can help reporters move from a promising question to a clean nut graf, from a messy draft to a coherent structure, and from “interesting” to “compelling and useful.” Comfort with the realities of web publishing: you understand urgency and speed, without sacrificing accuracy and care. Enthusiasm for (and ideally familiarity with) Future Perfect’s core areas — emerging technology and AI, philanthropy, global health, global development, and animal welfare — and a genuine appetite for evidence-driven reporting and argument. An organized, self-starting approach: you can manage multiple moving parts (fellows, edits, training, recruitment, newsletters) without dropping the thread. WHERE YOU’LL WORK This job is based in DC or NYC. WHY VOX MEDIA? WHAT WE OFFER This is a permanent, full-time position. This job is benefits-eligible. We pride ourselves in providing comprehensive benefits to support all of our employees wherever they are in life. You can find more information about our benefits here. OUR DEI+ COMMITMENT Vox Media is committed to building an inclusive environment where everyone can show up as their authentic selves and create their best work. title: Senior Video Producer, Unexplainable
description: Back to jobsNewSenior Video Producer, UnexplainableNew York, NYApplyVox is seeking a technically skilled, creative, and organized Senior Video Producer to develop and produce a weekly video interview version of Unexplainable, Vox’s award-winning narrative podcast. This is a one-year benefits eligible contract position. Unexplainable is a sound-rich biweekly podcast that delves into the mysteries of science with equal parts playfulness and journalistic rigor. We are looking for an experienced video producer to help us translate the show to video through engaging visuals, thoughtful editing, and journalistic best practices.The video producer will collaborate closely with the Unexplainable team to make a stand-out weekly video podcast. This is an exciting opportunity to help us develop a new visual identity for our audience. You’ll be involved in all steps of production, from initial brainstorms, planning and coordinating recordings, editing, and managing post-production to publishing. You’ll help develop the visual identity of the show, and be responsible for assembling, refining, and packaging each episode in Premiere. This is a highly collaborative role. You’ll create content cuts for both video and audio versions of the podcast, and you’ll work closely with producer-hosts, story editors, and audio engineers, as well as a video editor and animator. Ideally, you bring with you a background in journalism, a curiosity about science, a strong editorial sensibility, and a great deal of excitement about figuring out how to translate a narrative audio podcast to video. Precision, curiosity, confident editorial judgement, and strong technical instincts are also key to succeeding in this role. To apply, please submit a cover letter clearly outlining how your skills and experience meet or exceed the key candidate requirements. Include links to 3-5 videos that demonstrate your ability to produce original digital video shows and podcasts. Bonus points awarded for exhibiting some awareness of Unexplainable podcast episodes. WHAT YOU’LL DO Lead the full video and audio editing workflow for each episode, shaping structure, pacing, clarity, and visual presentation. Manage multi-track/multi-camera assembly, layout adjustments, sync, and cleanup; use Premiere/After Effects for video. Record and remotely direct host video; manage technical logistics for remote and some studio recordings (Riverside or similar). Prepare assets — thumbnails, captions, titles, descriptions — and handle uploads, scheduling, metadata, and QC for consistent platform presentation. Manage multiple episodes at once while maintaining accuracy, organization, and deadlines. WHO YOU ARE At least 3 to 5 years of professional experience producing really good videos, ideally for longform, highly produced, interview-driven, or idea-focused shows. Expert-level understanding of Adobe Suite, especially Premiere and After Effects. Experience with Descript and ProTools is a plus. Experience with publishing best practices—including thumbnails, titles, descriptions, tags, and performance optimization. Proficiency with studio lighting setups and field shoots. Familiarity with long-form conversational editing, especially intellectually rich or exploratory discussions. An interest or background in science and narrative journalism. If you think you have what it takes, but don't meet every single point in our job posting, please apply with a cover letter to let us know how you believe you can bring your unique skills to the Vox Media team or get in touch! Slots™, Wonka Slots™, Top Eleven™, Toon Blast™, Empires & Puzzles™, Merge Dragons!™, CSR Racing™, Harry Potter: Puzzles & Spells™, Match Factory™, and Color Block Jam™—plus many more! Whether you're spinning, strategizing, matching, or competing, Zynga is where fun meets innovation—and where you can take your career to the next level.
